Parameters.Name: AccessTokens

Description: This resource configures an Hosted Content Filter Rule in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the policy that you want to modify. There is only one policy named 'Default'
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.
Parameters.Name: AdminDisplayName

Parameters.Description: The BccSuspiciousOutboundAdditionalRecipients parameter specifies the recipients to add to the Bcc field of outgoing spam messages. Valid input for this parameter is an email address. Separate multiple email addresses with commas.
Parameters.Name: BccSuspiciousOutboundAdditionalRecipients

Parameters.Description: The BccSuspiciousOutboundMail parameter enables or disables adding recipients to the Bcc field of outgoing spam messages. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false. The default value is $false. You specify the additional recipients using the BccSuspiciousOutboundAdditionalRecipients parameter.
Parameters.Name: BccSuspiciousOutboundMail

Parameters.Description: The NotifyOutboundSpam parameter enables or disables sending notification messages to administrators when an outgoing message is determined to be spam. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false. The default value is $false. You specify the administrators to notify by using the NotifyOutboundSpamRecipients parameter.
Parameters.Name: NotifyOutboundSpam

Parameters.Description: The NotifyOutboundSpamRecipients parameter specifies the administrators to notify when an outgoing message is determined to be spam. Valid input for this parameter is an email address. Separate multiple email addresses with commas.
Parameters.Name: NotifyOutboundSpamRecipients

Parameters.Description: The RecipientLimitInternalPerHour parameter specifies the maximum number of internal recipients that a user can send to within an hour. A valid value is 0 to 10000. The default value is 0, which means the service defaults are used.
Parameters.Name: RecipientLimitInternalPerHour

Parameters.Description: The RecipientLimitPerDay parameter specifies the maximum number of recipients that a user can send to within a day. A valid value is 0 to 10000. The default value is 0, which means the service defaults are used.
Parameters.Name: RecipientLimitPerDay

Parameters.Description: The RecipientLimitExternalPerHour parameter specifies the maximum number of external recipients that a user can send to within an hour. A valid value is 0 to 10000. The default value is 0, which means the service defaults are used.
Parameters.Name: RecipientLimitExternalPerHour

Parameters.Description: The ActionWhenThresholdReached parameter specifies the action to take when any of the limits specified in the policy are reached. Valid values are: Alert, BlockUser, BlockUserForToday. BlockUserForToday is the default value.
Parameters.Name: ActionWhenThresholdReached

Parameters.Description: The AutoForwardingMode specifies how the policy controls automatic email forwarding to outbound recipients. Valid values are: Automatic, On, Off.
Parameters.Name: AutoForwardingMode

Parameters.Description: Specify if this policy should exist or not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Description: The HostedOutboundSpamFilterPolicy parameter specifies the name of the HostedOutboundSpamFilter policy that's associated with the HostedOutboundSpamFilter rule.
Parameters.Name: HostedOutboundSpamFilterPolicy

Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should be enabled. Default is $true.

Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.
Parameters.Name: Priority

Parameters.Description: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for senders with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFrom parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFrom

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent by group members. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The SenderDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for senders with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: SenderDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The From parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.
Parameters.Name: From

Parameters.Description: The FromMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent by group members. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: FromMemberOf

Description: This resource configures an Hosted Content Filter Rule in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the outbound connector that you want to modify.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The AssociatedAcceptedDomains parameter specifies the accepted domains that the connector applies to, thereby limiting its scope. For example, you can apply the connector to a specific accepted domain in your organization, such as contoso.com.
Parameters.Name: AssociatedAcceptedDomains

Parameters.Description: The CloudServicesMailEnabled parameter specifies whether the connector is used for hybrid mail flow between an on-premises Exchange environment and Microsoft Office 365. Specifically, this parameter controls how certain internal X-MS-Exchange-Organization-* message headers are handled in messages that are sent between accepted domains in the on-premises and cloud organizations. These headers are collectively known as cross-premises headers. DO NOT USE MANUALLY!
Parameters.Name: CloudServicesMailEnabled

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorSource parameter specifies how the connector is created. DO NOT CHANGE THIS!
Parameters.Name: ConnectorSource

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorType parameter specifies a category for the domains that are serviced by the connector. Valid values are Partner and OnPremises
Parameters.Name: ConnectorType

Parameters.Description: The EFSkipIPs parameter specifies the source IP addresses to skip in Enhanced Filtering for Connectors when the EFSkipLastIP parameter value is $false.
Parameters.Name: EFSkipIPs

Parameters.Description: The EFSkipLastIP parameter specifies the behavior of Enhanced Filtering for Connectors.
Parameters.Name: EFSkipLastIP

Parameters.Description: The EFUsers parameter specifies the recipients that Enhanced Filtering for Connectors applies to.
Parameters.Name: EFUsers

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether connector is enabled.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: The RequireTLS parameter specifies that all messages received by this connector require TLS transmission. Valid values for this parameter are $true or $false. The default value is $false. When the RequireTLS parameter is set to $true, all messages received by this connector require TLS transmission.
Parameters.Name: RequireTls

Parameters.Description: The RestrictDomainsToCertificate parameter specifies that Office 365 should identify incoming messages that are eligible for this connector by verifying that the remote server authenticates using a TLS certificate that has the TlsSenderCertificateName in the Subject.
Parameters.Name: RestrictDomainsToCertificate

Parameters.Description: The RestrictDomainsToIPAddresses parameter, when set to $true, automatically rejects mail from the domains specified by the SenderDomains parameter if the mail originates from an IP address that isn't specified by the SenderIPAddresses parameter.
Parameters.Name: RestrictDomainsToIPAddresses

Parameters.Description: The SenderDomains parameter specifies the remote domains from which this connector accepts messages, thereby limiting its scope. You can use a wildcard character to specify all subdomains of a specified domain, as shown in the following example: *.contoso.com. However, you can't embed a wildcard character, as shown in the following example: domain.*.contoso.com.
Parameters.Name: SenderDomains

Parameters.Description: The SenderIPAddresses parameter specifies the remote IP addresses from which this connector accepts messages.
Parameters.Name: SenderIPAddresses

Parameters.Description: The TlsSenderCertificateName parameter specifies the certificate used by the sender's domain when the RequireTls parameter is set to $true. Valid input for the TlsSenderCertificateName parameter is an SMTP domain.
Parameters.Name: TlsSenderCertificateName

Parameters.Description: The TreatMessagesAsInternal parameter specifies an alternative method to identify messages sent from an on-premises organization as internal messages. You should only consider using this parameter when your on-premises organization doesn't use Exchange.
Parameters.Name: TreatMessagesAsInternal

Parameters.Description: Specifies if this Outbound connector should exist.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath

Parameters.Name: AccessTokens

Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The DiscoveryEndpoint parameter specifies the externally-accessible URL that's used for the Autodiscover service for the domain that's configured in the Intra-Organization connector.
Parameters.Name: DiscoveryEndpoint

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether connector is enabled.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: The TargetAddressDomains parameter specifies the domain namespaces that will be used in the Intra-organization connector. These domains must have valid Autodiscover endpoints defined in their organizations. The domains and their associated Autodiscover endpoints are used by the Intra-Organization connector for feature and service connectivity. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: TargetAddressDomains

Parameters.Description: The TargetSharingEpr parameter specifies the URL of the target Exchange Web Services that will be used in the Intra-Organization connector.
Parameters.Name: TargetSharingEpr

Parameters.Description: Specifies if this Intra-Organization connector should exist.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Description: Create a new EXOIntraOrganizationConnector in your cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.
Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance

Parameters.Description: The AutomaticServiceUpdateEnabled parameter specifies whether to allow the automatic addition of new features within Azure Information Protection for your cloud-based organization.
Parameters.Name: AutomaticServiceUpdateEnabled

Parameters.Description: The AzureRMSLicensingEnabled parameter specifies whether the Exchange Online organization can to connect directly to Azure Rights Management.
Parameters.Name: AzureRMSLicensingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DecryptAttachmentForEncryptOnly parameter specifies whether mail recipients have unrestricted rights on the attachment or not for Encrypt-only mails sent using Microsoft Purview Message Encryption.
Parameters.Name: DecryptAttachmentForEncryptOnly

Parameters.Description: The EDiscoverySuperUserEnabled parameter specifies whether members of the Discovery Management role group can access IRM-protected messages in a discovery mailbox that were returned by a discovery search.
Parameters.Name: EDiscoverySuperUserEnabled

Parameters.Description: The EnablePdfEncryption parameter specifies whether to enable the encryption of PDF attachments using Microsoft Purview Message Encryption.
Parameters.Name: EnablePdfEncryption

Parameters.Description: The InternalLicensingEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable IRM features for messages that are sent to internal and external recipients.
Parameters.Name: InternalLicensingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The JournalReportDecryptionEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable journal report decryption.
Parameters.Name: JournalReportDecryptionEnabled

Parameters.Description: The LicensingLocation parameter specifies the RMS licensing URLs. You can specify multiple URL values separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: LicensingLocation

Parameters.Description: This parameter is available only in the cloud-based service.
Parameters.Name: RejectIfRecipientHasNoRights

Parameters.Description: The RMSOnlineKeySharingLocation parameter specifies the Azure Rights Management URL that's used to get the trusted publishing domain (TPD) for the Exchange Online organization.
Parameters.Name: RMSOnlineKeySharingLocation

Parameters.Description: The SearchEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable searching of IRM-encrypted messages in Outlook on the web (formerly known as Outlook Web App).
Parameters.Name: SearchEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SimplifiedClientAccessDoNotForwardDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable Do not forward in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: SimplifiedClientAccessDoNotForwardDisabled

Parameters.Description: The SimplifiedClientAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable the Protect button in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: SimplifiedClientAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SimplifiedClientAccessEncryptOnlyDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable Encrypt only in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: SimplifiedClientAccessEncryptOnlyDisabled

Parameters.Description: The TransportDecryptionSetting parameter specifies the transport decryption configuration.
Parameters.Name: TransportDecryptionSetting

Description: Modify the resource Configuration policy in your cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Description: Name of the Journal Rule
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The JournalEmailAddress parameter specifies a recipient object to which journal reports are sent. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: JournalEmailAddress

Parameters.Description: The Recipient parameter specifies the SMTP address of a mailbox, contact, or distribution group to journal. If you specify a distribution group, all recipients in that distribution group are journaled. All messages sent to or from a recipient are journaled.
Parameters.Name: Recipient

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the Journal Rule is enabled or not.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: The Scope parameter specifies the scope of email messages to which the journal rule is applied
Parameters.Name: RuleScope

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the rule exists, Absent that it does not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: ExternalEmailAddress

Parameters.Description: The Alias parameter specifies the Exchange alias (also known as the mail nickname) for the recipient. This value identifies the recipient as a mail-enabled object, and shouldn't be confused with multiple email addresses for the same recipient (also known as proxy addresses). A recipient can have only one Alias value. The maximum length is 64 characters.
Parameters.Name: Alias

Parameters.Description: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name of the mail contact. The display name is visible in the Exchange admin center and in address lists.

Parameters.Name: MacAttachmentFormat

Parameters.Description: The MessageBodyFormat parameter specifies the message body format for messages sent to the mail contact or mail user. Valid values are: Text, Html, TextAndHtml
Parameters.Name: MessageBodyFormat

Parameters.Description: The MessageFormat parameter specifies the message format for messages sent to the mail contact or mail user. Valid values are: Mime, Text
Parameters.Name: MessageFormat

Parameters.Description: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this mail contact. A moderator approves messages sent to the mail contact before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization.

Parameters.Description: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.
Parameters.Name: ModerationEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SendModerationNotifications parameter specifies when moderation notification messages are sent. Valid values are: ALways, Internal, Never

Parameters.Description: The CustomExternalBody parameter specifies the body of the custom notification message for malware detections in messages from external senders. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Parameters.Name: CustomFromName

Parameters.Name: CustomInternalBody

Parameters.Description: The CustomNotifications parameter enables or disables custom notification messages for malware detections in messages from internal or external senders. Valid values are: $true, $false.

Parameters.Description: The EnableExternalSenderAdminNotifications parameter enables or disables sending malware detection notification messages to an administrator for messages from external senders. Valid values are: $true, $false.

Parameters.Description: The FileTypes parameter specifies the file types that are automatically blocked by common attachment blocking (also known as the Common Attachment Types Filter), regardless of content.

Parameters.Description: The QuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined as malware.
Parameters.Name: QuarantineTag

Parameters.Description: The ZapEnabled parameter enables or disables zero-hour auto purge (ZAP) for malware. ZAP detects malware in unread messages that have already been delivered to the user's Inbox. Valid values are: $true, $false.
Parameters.Name: ZapEnabled

Description: Create or modify a EXOMalwareFilterPolicy in your cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the EXO resource you want to modify.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.
Parameters.Name: Comments

Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter enables or disables the malware filter rule. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false. The default value is $true.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The MalwareFilterPolicy parameter specifies the malware filter policy to apply to messages that match the conditions defined by this malware filter rule.
Parameters.Name: MalwareFilterPolicy

Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.
Parameters.Name: Priority

Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: SentTo

Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf

Description: This resource configures RBAC Management Roles in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies a name for the new management role assignment. The maximum length of the name is 64 characters.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The Role parameter specifies the existing role to assign. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the role.
Parameters.Name: Role

Parameters.Description: The App parameter specifies the service principal to assign the management role to. Specifically, the ServiceId GUID value from the output of the Get-ServicePrincipal cmdlet (for example, 6233fba6-0198-4277-892f-9275bf728bcc).
Parameters.Name: App

Parameters.Description: The Policy parameter specifies the name of the management role assignment policy to assign the management role to.
Parameters.Name: Policy

Parameters.Description: The SecurityGroup parameter specifies the name of the management role group or mail-enabled universal security group to assign the management role to.
Parameters.Name: SecurityGroup

Parameters.Description: The User parameter specifies the name or alias of the user to assign the management role to.
Parameters.Name: User

Parameters.Description: The CustomRecipientWriteScope parameter specifies the existing recipient-based management scope to associate with this management role assignment.
Parameters.Name: CustomRecipientWriteScope

Parameters.Description: The CustomResourceScope parameter specifies the custom management scope to associate with this management role assignment. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the management scope.
Parameters.Name: CustomResourceScope

Parameters.Description: The ExclusiveConfigWriteScope parameter specifies the exclusive configuration-based management scope to associate with the new role assignment.
Parameters.Name: ExclusiveRecipientWriteScope

Parameters.Description: The RecipientAdministrativeUnitScope parameter specifies the administrative unit to scope the new role assignment to.
Parameters.Name: RecipientAdministrativeUnitScope

Parameters.Description: The RecipientOrganizationalUnitScope parameter specifies the OU to scope the new role assignment to. If you use the RecipientOrganizationalUnitScope parameter, you can't use the CustomRecipientWriteScope or ExclusiveRecipientWriteScope parameters.
Parameters.Name: RecipientOrganizationalUnitScope

Parameters.Description: The RecipientRelativeWriteScope parameter specifies the type of restriction to apply to a recipient scope. The available types are None, Organization, MyGAL, Self, and MyDistributionGroups. The RecipientRelativeWriteScope parameter is automatically set when the CustomRecipientWriteScope or RecipientOrganizationalUnitScope parameters are used.
Parameters.Name: RecipientRelativeWriteScope

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the friendly name of the mobile device mailbox policy.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The AllowApplePushNotifications parameter specifies whether push notifications are allowed to Apple mobile devices.
Parameters.Name: AllowApplePushNotifications

Parameters.Description: The AllowBluetooth parameter specifies whether the Bluetooth capabilities are allowed on the mobile phone. The available options are Disable, HandsfreeOnly, and Allow. The default value is Allow.
Parameters.Name: AllowBluetooth

Parameters.Description: The AllowBrowser parameter indicates whether Microsoft Pocket Internet Explorer is allowed on the mobile phone. This parameter doesn't affect third-party browsers.
Parameters.Name: AllowBrowser

Parameters.Description: The AllowCamera parameter specifies whether the mobile phone's camera is allowed.
Parameters.Name: AllowCamera

Parameters.Description: The AllowConsumerEmail parameter specifies whether the mobile phone user can configure a personal email account on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowConsumerEmail

Parameters.Description: The AllowDesktopSync parameter specifies whether the mobile phone can synchronize with a desktop computer through a cable.
Parameters.Name: AllowDesktopSync

Parameters.Description: The AllowExternalDeviceManagement parameter specifies whether an external device management program is allowed to manage the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowExternalDeviceManagement

Parameters.Description: The AllowGooglePushNotifications parameter controls whether the user can receive push notifications from Google for Outlook on the web for devices.
Parameters.Name: AllowGooglePushNotifications

Parameters.Description: The AllowHTMLEmail parameter specifies whether HTML email is enabled on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowHTMLEmail

Parameters.Description: The AllowInternetSharing parameter specifies whether the mobile phone can be used as a modem to connect a computer to the Internet.
Parameters.Name: AllowInternetSharing

Parameters.Description: The AllowIrDA parameter specifies whether infrared connections are allowed to the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowIrDA

Parameters.Description: The AllowMobileOTAUpdate parameter specifies whether the Exchange ActiveSync mailbox policy can be sent to the mobile phone over a cellular data connection.
Parameters.Name: AllowMobileOTAUpdate

Parameters.Description: The AllowMicrosoftPushNotifications parameter specifies whether push notifications are enabled on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: AllowMicrosoftPushNotifications

Parameters.Description: The AllowNonProvisionableDevices parameter specifies whether all mobile phones can synchronize with the server running Exchange.
Parameters.Name: AllowNonProvisionableDevices

Parameters.Description: The AllowPOPIMAPEmail parameter specifies whether the user can configure a POP3 or IMAP4 email account on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowPOPIMAPEmail

Parameters.Description: The AllowRemoteDesktop parameter specifies whether the mobile phone can initiate a remote desktop connection.
Parameters.Name: AllowRemoteDesktop

Parameters.Description: The AllowSimplePassword parameter specifies whether a simple device password is allowed. A simple device password is a password that has a specific pattern, such as 1111 or 1234.
Parameters.Name: AllowSimplePassword

Parameters.Description: The AllowSMIMEEncryptionAlgorithmNegotiation parameter specifies whether the messaging application on the mobile device can negotiate the encryption algorithm if a recipient's certificate doesn't support the specified encryption algorithm.
Parameters.Name: AllowSMIMEEncryptionAlgorithmNegotiation

Parameters.Description: The AllowSMIMESoftCerts parameter specifies whether S/MIME software certificates are allowed.
Parameters.Name: AllowSMIMESoftCerts

Parameters.Description: The AllowStorageCard parameter specifies whether the mobile phone can access information stored on a storage card.
Parameters.Name: AllowStorageCard

Parameters.Description: The AllowTextMessaging parameter specifies whether text messaging is allowed from the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowTextMessaging

Parameters.Description: The AllowUnsignedApplications parameter specifies whether unsigned applications can be installed on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowUnsignedApplications

Parameters.Description: The AllowUnsignedInstallationPackages parameter specifies whether unsigned installation packages can be executed on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowUnsignedInstallationPackages

Parameters.Description: The AllowWiFi parameter specifies whether wireless Internet access is allowed on the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowWiFi

Parameters.Description: The AlphanumericPasswordRequired parameter specifies whether the password for the mobile phone must be alphanumeric.
Parameters.Name: AlphanumericPasswordRequired

Parameters.Description: The ApprovedApplicationList parameter specifies a list of approved applications for the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: ApprovedApplicationList

Parameters.Description: The AttachmentsEnabled parameter specifies whether attachments can be downloaded.
Parameters.Name: AttachmentsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DeviceEncryptionEnabled parameter specifies whether encryption is enabled.
Parameters.Name: DeviceEncryptionEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DevicePolicyRefreshInterval parameter specifies how often the policy is sent from the server to the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: DevicePolicyRefreshInterval

Parameters.Description: The IrmEnabled parameter specifies whether Information Rights Management (IRM) is enabled for the mailbox policy.
Parameters.Name: IrmEnabled

Parameters.Description: The IsDefault parameter specifies whether this policy is the default Mobile Device mailbox policy.
Parameters.Name: IsDefault

Parameters.Description: The MaxAttachmentSize parameter specifies the maximum size of attachments that can be downloaded to the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: MaxAttachmentSize

Parameters.Description: The MaxCalendarAgeFilter parameter specifies the maximum range of calendar days that can be synchronized to the device.
Parameters.Name: MaxCalendarAgeFilter

Parameters.Description: The MaxEmailAgeFilter parameter specifies the maximum number of days of email items to synchronize to the mobile phone.
Parameters.Name: MaxEmailAgeFilter

Parameters.Description: The MaxEmailBodyTruncationSize parameter specifies the maximum size at which email messages are truncated when synchronized to the mobile phone. The value is specified in kilobytes (KB).
Parameters.Name: MaxEmailBodyTruncationSize

Parameters.Description: The MaxEmailHTMLBodyTruncationSize parameter specifies the maximum size at which HTML-formatted email messages are synchronized to the mobile phone. The value is specified in KB.
Parameters.Name: MaxEmailHTMLBodyTruncationSize

Parameters.Description: The MaxInactivityTimeDeviceLock parameter specifies the length of time that the mobile phone can be inactive before the password is required to reactivate it.
Parameters.Name: MaxInactivityTimeLock

Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordFailedAttempts parameter specifies the number of attempts a user can make to enter the correct password for the mobile phone. You can enter any number from 4 through 16 or the value Unlimited.
Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordFailedAttempts

Parameters.Description: The MinPasswordComplexCharacters parameter specifies the character sets that are required in the password of the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: MinPasswordComplexCharacters

Parameters.Description: The MinPasswordLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters in the mobile device password.
Parameters.Name: MinPasswordLength

Parameters.Description: The PasswordEnabled parameter specifies whether a password is required on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: PasswordEnabled

Parameters.Description: The PasswordExpiration parameter specifies how long a password can be used on a mobile device before the user is forced to change the password.
Parameters.Name: PasswordExpiration

Parameters.Description: The PasswordHistory parameter specifies the number of unique new passwords that need to be created on the mobile device before an old password can be reused.
Parameters.Name: PasswordHistory

Parameters.Description: The PasswordRecoveryEnabled parameter specifies whether the recovery password for the mobile device is stored in Exchange.
Parameters.Name: PasswordRecoveryEnabled

Parameters.Description: The RequireDeviceEncryption parameter specifies whether encryption is required on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: RequireDeviceEncryption

Parameters.Description: The RequireEncryptedSMIMEMessages parameter specifies whether the mobile device must send encrypted S/MIME messages.
Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptedSMIMEMessages

Parameters.Description: The RequireEncryptionSMIMEAlgorithm parameter specifies the algorithm that's required to encrypt S/MIME messages on a mobile device.
Parameters.Name: RequireEncryptionSMIMEAlgorithm

Parameters.Description: The RequireSignedSMIMEAlgorithm parameter specifies the algorithm that's used to sign S/MIME messages on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: RequireManualSyncWhenRoaming

Parameters.Description: The RequireSignedSMIMEAlgorithm parameter specifies the algorithm that's used to sign S/MIME messages on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: RequireSignedSMIMEAlgorithm

Parameters.Description: The RequireSignedSMIMEMessages parameter specifies whether the mobile device must send signed S/MIME messages.
Parameters.Name: RequireSignedSMIMEMessages

Parameters.Description: The RequireStorageCardEncryption parameter specifies whether storage card encryption is required on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: RequireStorageCardEncryption

Parameters.Description: The UnapprovedInROMApplicationList parameter specifies a list of applications that can't be run in ROM on the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: UnapprovedInROMApplicationList

Parameters.Description: The UNCAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows file shares is enabled from the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: UNCAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The WSSAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows SharePoint Services is enabled from the mobile device.
Parameters.Name: WSSAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The HybridDomains parameter specifies the domains that are configured in the hybrid deployment between an Office 365 tenant and an on-premises Exchange organization. The domains specified in this parameter must match the domains listed in the HybridConfiguration Active Directory object for the on-premises Exchange organization configured by the Hybrid Configuration wizard.
Parameters.Name: HybridDomains

Parameters.Description: The InboundConnector parameter specifies the name of the inbound connector configured on the Microsoft Exchange Online Protection (EOP) service for a hybrid deployment configured with an on-premises Exchange organization.
Parameters.Name: InboundConnector

Parameters.Description: The OutboundConnector parameter specifies the name of the outbound connector configured on the EOP service for a hybrid deployment configured with an on-premises Exchange organization.
Parameters.Name: OutboundConnector

Parameters.Description: The OrganizationName parameter specifies the Active Directory object name of the on-premises Exchange organization.
Parameters.Name: OrganizationName

Parameters.Description: The OrganizationGuid parameter specifies the globally unique identifier (GUID) of the on-premises Exchange organization object in the Office 365 tenant.
Parameters.Name: OrganizationGuid

Parameters.Description: The OrganizationRelationship parameter specifies the organization relationship configured by the Hybrid Configuration wizard on the Office 365 tenant as part of a hybrid deployment with an on-premises Exchange organization. This organization relationship defines the federated sharing features enabled on the Office 365 tenant.
Parameters.Name: OrganizationRelationship

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: Specify if the On-Premises Organization should exist or not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.
Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.
Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword
Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures On-Premises Organization in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.
Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance

Parameters.Description: The ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutEnabled parameter specifies whether the timed logoff feature is enabled. The default value is $true
Parameters.Name: ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutInterval parameter specifies the time span for logoff. You enter this value as a time span: hh:mm:ss where hh = hours, mm = minutes and ss = seconds. Valid values for this parameter are from 00:05:00 to 08:00:00 (5 minutes to 8 hours). The default value is 06:00:00 (6 hours).
Parameters.Name: ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutInterval

Parameters.Description: The ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutWithSingleSignOnEnabled parameter specifies whether to keep single sign-on enabled. The default value is $true.
Parameters.Name: ActivityBasedAuthenticationTimeoutWithSingleSignOnEnabled

Parameters.Description: The AppsForOfficeEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable apps for Outlook features. By default, the parameter is set to $true. If the flag is set to $false, no new apps can be activated for any user in the organization.
Parameters.Name: AppsForOfficeEnabled

Parameters.Description: The AsyncSendEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable async send in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: AsyncSendEnabled

Parameters.Description: The AuditDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable or enable mailbox auditing for the organization.
Parameters.Name: AuditDisabled

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter to $true will cause unknown users to be redirected to the on-premises endpoint and will allow on-premises users to discover their mailbox automatically.
Parameters.Name: AutodiscoverPartialDirSync

Parameters.Description: The AutoExpandingArchive switch enables the unlimited archiving feature (called auto-expanding archiving) in an Exchange Online organization. You don't need to specify a value with this switch.
Parameters.Name: AutoExpandingArchive

Parameters.Description: No description available for BlockMoveMessagesForGroupFolders
Parameters.Name: BlockMoveMessagesForGroupFolders

Parameters.Description: The BookingsAddressEntryRestricted parameter specifies whether addresses can be collected from Bookings customers.
Parameters.Name: BookingsAddressEntryRestricted

Parameters.Description: The BookingsAuthEnabled parameter specifies whether to enforce authentication to access all published Bookings pages.
Parameters.Name: BookingsAuthEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsBlockedWordsEnabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsBlockedWordsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BookingsCreationOfCustomQuestionsRestricted parameter specifies whether Bookings admins can add custom questions.
Parameters.Name: BookingsCreationOfCustomQuestionsRestricted

Parameters.Description: The BookingsEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable Microsoft Bookings in an organization.
Parameters.Name: BookingsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BookingsExposureOfStaffDetailsRestricted parameter specifies whether the attributes of internal Bookings staff members are visible to external Bookings customers.
Parameters.Name: BookingsExposureOfStaffDetailsRestricted

Parameters.Description: The BookingsMembershipApprovalRequired parameter enables a membership approval requirement when new staff members are added to Bookings calendars.
Parameters.Name: BookingsMembershipApprovalRequired

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsNamingPolicyEnabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsNamingPolicyEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsNamingPolicyPrefix
Parameters.Name: BookingsNamingPolicyPrefix

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsNamingPolicyPrefixEnabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsNamingPolicyPrefixEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsNamingPolicySuffix
Parameters.Name: BookingsNamingPolicySuffix

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsNamingPolicySuffixEnabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsNamingPolicySuffixEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BookingsNotesEntryRestricted parameter specifies whether appointment notes can be collected from Bookings customers.
Parameters.Name: BookingsNotesEntryRestricted

Parameters.Description: The BookingsPaymentsEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable online payment node inside Bookings.
Parameters.Name: BookingsPaymentsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BookingsPhoneNumberEntryRestricted parameter specifies whether phone numbers can be collected from Bookings customers.
Parameters.Name: BookingsPhoneNumberEntryRestricted

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsSearchEngineIndexDisabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsSearchEngineIndexDisabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for BookingsSmsMicrosoftEnabled
Parameters.Name: BookingsSmsMicrosoftEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BookingsSocialSharingRestricted parameter allows you to control whether, or not, your users can see social sharing options inside Bookings.
Parameters.Name: BookingsSocialSharingRestricted

Parameters.Description: The ByteEncoderTypeFor7BitCharsets parameter specifies the 7-bit transfer encoding method for MIME format for messages sent to this remote domain.
Parameters.Name: ByteEncoderTypeFor7BitCharsets

Parameters.Description: No description available for ComplianceMLBgdCrawlEnabled
Parameters.Name: ComplianceMLBgdCrawlEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsActionableMessagesEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable actionable buttons in messages (connector cards) from connected apps on Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsActionableMessagesEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable all connected apps in organization.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsEnabledForOutlook parameter specifies whether to enable or disable connected apps in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsEnabledForOutlook

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsEnabledForSharepoint parameter specifies whether to enable or disable connected apps on Sharepoint.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsEnabledForSharepoint

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsEnabledForTeams parameter specifies whether to enable or disable connected apps on Teams.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsEnabledForTeams

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorsEnabledForYammer parameter specifies whether to enable or disable connected apps on Yammer.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorsEnabledForYammer

Parameters.Description: Enable Customer Lockbox.
Parameters.Name: CustomerLockboxEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DefaultAuthenticationPolicy parameter specifies the authentication policy that's used for the whole organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the policy.
Parameters.Name: DefaultAuthenticationPolicy

Parameters.Description: The DefaultGroupAccessType parameter specifies the default access type for Office 365 groups.
Parameters.Name: DefaultGroupAccessType

Parameters.Description: The DefaultMinutesToReduceLongEventsBy parameter specifies the number of minutes to reduce calendar events by if the events are 60 minutes or longer.
Parameters.Name: DefaultMinutesToReduceLongEventsBy

Parameters.Description: The DefaultMinutesToReduceShortEventsBy parameter specifies the number of minutes to reduce calendar events by if the events are less than 60 minutes long.
Parameters.Name: DefaultMinutesToReduceShortEventsBy

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderAgeLimit parameter specifies the default age limit for the contents of public folders across the entire organization. Content in a public folder is automatically deleted when this age limit is exceeded. This attribute applies to all public folders in the organization that don't have their own AgeLimit setting. To specify a value, enter it as a time span: dd.hh:mm:ss where d = days, h = hours, m = minutes, and s = seconds. Or, enter the value $null. The default value is blank ($null).
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderAgeLimit

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderDeletedItemRetention parameter specifies the default value of the length of time to retain deleted items for public folders across the entire organization. This attribute applies to all public folders in the organization that don't have their own RetainDeletedItemsFor attribute set.
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderDeletedItemRetention

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderIssueWarningQuota parameter specifies the default value across the entire organization for the public folder size at which a warning message is sent to this folder's owners, warning that the public folder is almost full. This attribute applies to all public folders within the organization that don't have their own warning quota attribute set. The default value of this attribute is unlimited. The valid input range for this parameter is from 0 through 2199023254529 bytes(2 TB). If you enter a value of unlimited, no size limit is imposed on the public folder.
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderIssueWarningQuota

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderMaxItemSize parameter specifies the default maximum size for posted items within public folders across the entire organization. Items larger than the value of the DefaultPublicFolderMaxItemSize parameter are rejected. This attribute applies to all public folders within the organization that don't have their own MaxItemSize attribute set. The default value of this attribute is unlimited.
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderMaxItemSize

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderMovedItemRetention parameter specifies how long items that have been moved between mailboxes are kept in the source mailbox for recovery purposes before being removed by the Public Folder Assistant.
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderMovedItemRetention

Parameters.Description: The DefaultPublicFolderProhibitPostQuota parameter specifies the size of a public folder at which users are notified that the public folder is full. Users can't post to a folder whose size is larger than the DefaultPublicFolderProhibitPostQuota parameter value. The default value of this attribute is unlimited.
Parameters.Name: DefaultPublicFolderProhibitPostQuota

Parameters.Description: The DelayedDelicensingEnabled parameter enables or disables a 30 day grace period for Exchange Online license removals from mailboxes.
Parameters.Name: DelayedDelicensingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DirectReportsGroupAutoCreationEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the automatic creation of direct report Office 365 groups.
Parameters.Name: DirectReportsGroupAutoCreationEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DisablePlusAddressInRecipients parameter specifies whether to enable or disable plus addressing (also known as subaddressing) for Exchange Online mailboxes.
Parameters.Name: DisablePlusAddressInRecipients

Parameters.Description: The DistributionGroupDefaultOU parameter specifies the container where distribution groups are created by default.
Parameters.Name: DistributionGroupDefaultOU

Parameters.Description: The DistributionGroupNameBlockedWordsList parameter specifies words that can't be included in the names of distribution groups. Separate multiple values with commas.
Parameters.Name: DistributionGroupNameBlockedWordsList

Parameters.Description: The DistributionGroupNamingPolicy parameter specifies the template applied to the name of distribution groups that are created in the organization. You can enforce that a prefix or suffix be applied to all distribution groups. Prefixes and suffixes can be either a string or an attribute, and you can combine strings and attributes.
Parameters.Name: DistributionGroupNamingPolicy

Parameters.Description: The ElcProcessingDisabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the processing of mailboxes by the Managed Folder Assistant.
Parameters.Name: ElcProcessingDisabled

Parameters.Description: The EnableOutlookEvents parameter specifies whether Outlook or Outlook on the web automatically discovers events from email messages and adds them to user calendars.
Parameters.Name: EnableOutlookEvents

Parameters.Description: The EndUserDLUpgradeFlowsDisabled parameter specifies whether to prevent users from upgrading their own distribution groups to Office 365 groups in an Exchange Online organization.
Parameters.Name: EndUserDLUpgradeFlowsDisabled

Parameters.Description: The EndUserMailNotificationForDelayedDelicensingEnabled parameter enables or disables periodic email warnings to affected users that have pending Exchange Online license removal requests on their mailboxes.
Parameters.Name: EndUserMailNotificationForDelayedDelicensingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowEntourage parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Entourage 2008 to access Exchange Web Services (EWS) for the entire organization.
Parameters.Name: EwsAllowEntourage

Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowList parameter specifies the applications that are allowed to access EWS or REST when the EwsApplicationAccessPolicy parameter is set to EwsAllowList. Other applications that aren't specified by this parameter aren't allowed to access EWS or REST. You identify the application by its user agent string value. Wildcard characters (*) are supported.
Parameters.Name: EwsAllowList

Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowMacOutlook parameter enables or disables access to mailboxes by Outlook for Mac clients that use Exchange Web Services (for example, Outlook for Mac 2011 or later).
Parameters.Name: EwsAllowMacOutlook

Parameters.Description: The EwsAllowOutlook parameter enables or disables access to mailboxes by Outlook clients that use Exchange Web Services. Outlook uses Exchange Web Services for free/busy, out-of-office settings, and calendar sharing.
Parameters.Name: EwsAllowOutlook

Parameters.Description: The EwsApplicationAccessPolicy parameter specifies the client applications that have access to EWS and REST.
Parameters.Name: EwsApplicationAccessPolicy

Parameters.Description: The EwsBlockList parameter specifies the applications that aren't allowed to access EWS or REST when the EwsApplicationAccessPolicy parameter is set to EnforceBlockList. All other applications that aren't specified by this parameter are allowed to access EWS or REST. You identify the application by its user agent string value. Wildcard characters (*) are supported.
Parameters.Name: EwsBlockList

Parameters.Description: The EwsEnabled parameter specifies whether to globally enable or disable EWS access for the entire organization, regardless of what application is making the request.
Parameters.Name: EwsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeNotificationEnabled parameter enables or disables Exchange notifications sent to administrators regarding their organizations. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: ExchangeNotificationEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeNotificationRecipients parameter specifies the recipients for Exchange notifications sent to administrators regarding their organizations. If the ExchangeNotificationEnabled parameter is set to $false, no notification messages are sent. Be sure to enclose values that contain spaces in quotation marks and separate multiple values with commas. If this parameter isn't set, Exchange notifications are sent to all administrators.
Parameters.Name: ExchangeNotificationRecipients

Parameters.Description: The FindTimeAttendeeAuthenticationEnabled parameter controls whether attendees are required to verify their identity in meeting polls using the FindTime Outlook add-in.
Parameters.Name: FindTimeAttendeeAuthenticationEnabled

Parameters.Description: The FindTimeAutoScheduleDisabled parameter controls automatically scheduling the meeting once a consensus is reached in meeting polls using the FindTime Outlook add-in.
Parameters.Name: FindTimeAutoScheduleDisabled

Parameters.Description: The FindTimeLockPollForAttendeesEnabled controls whether the Lock poll for attendees setting is managed by the organization.
Parameters.Name: FindTimeLockPollForAttendeesEnabled

Parameters.Description: The FindTimeOnlineMeetingOptionDisabled parameter controls the availability of the Online meeting checkbox for Teams in meeting polls using the FindTime Outlook add-in.
Parameters.Name: FindTimeOnlineMeetingOptionDisabled

Parameters.Description: The FocusedInboxOn parameter enables or disables Focused Inbox for the organization.
Parameters.Name: FocusedInboxOn

Parameters.Description: The HierarchicalAddressBookRoot parameter specifies the user, contact, or group to be used as the root organization for a hierarchical address book in the Exchange organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: HierarchicalAddressBookRoot

Parameters.Description: The IPListBlocked parameter specifies the blocked IP addresses that aren't allowed to connect to Exchange Online organization. These settings affect client connections that use Basic authentication where on-premises Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS) servers federate authentication with Azure Active Directory. Note that the new settings might take up to 4 hours to fully propagate across the service.
Parameters.Name: IPListBlocked

Parameters.Description: No description available for IsGroupFoldersAndRulesEnabled
Parameters.Name: IsGroupFoldersAndRulesEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for IsGroupMemberAllowedToEditContent
Parameters.Name: IsGroupMemberAllowedToEditContent

Parameters.Description: The LeanPopoutEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable faster loading of pop-out messages in Outlook on the web for Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge.
Parameters.Name: LeanPopoutEnabled

Parameters.Description: The LinkPreviewEnabled parameter specifies whether link preview of URLs in email messages is allowed for the organization.
Parameters.Name: LinkPreviewEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsAllTipsEnabled parameter specifies whether MailTips are enabled. The default value is $true.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsAllTipsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsExternalRecipientsTipsEnabled parameter specifies whether MailTips for external recipients are enabled. The default value is $false.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsExternalRecipientsTipsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsGroupMetricsEnabled parameter specifies whether MailTips that rely on group metrics data are enabled. The default value is $true.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsGroupMetricsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsLargeAudienceThreshold parameter specifies what a large audience is. The default value is 25.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsLargeAudienceThreshold

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsMailboxSourcedTipsEnabled parameter specifies whether MailTips that rely on mailbox data (out-of-office or full mailbox) are enabled.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsMailboxSourcedTipsEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for MaskClientIpInReceivedHeadersEnabled.
Parameters.Name: MaskClientIpInReceivedHeadersEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for MatchSenderOrganizerProperties.
Parameters.Name: MatchSenderOrganizerProperties

Parameters.Description: No description available for MessageHighlightsEnabled.
Parameters.Name: MessageHighlightsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MessageRecallEnabled parameter enables or disables the message recall feature in the organization.
Parameters.Name: MessageRecallEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MessageRemindersEnabled parameter enables or disables the message reminders feature in the organization.
Parameters.Name: MessageRemindersEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MobileAppEducationEnabled specifies whether to show or hide the Outlook for iOS and Android education reminder in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: MobileAppEducationEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled parameter enables or disables modern authentication in the Exchange organization.
Parameters.Name: OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OnlineMeetingsByDefaultEnabled parameter specifies whether to set all meetings as Teams by default during meeting creation.
Parameters.Name: OnlineMeetingsByDefaultEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OutlookGifPickerDisabled parameter disables the GIF Search (powered by Bing) feature that's built into the Compose page in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: OutlookGifPickerDisabled

Parameters.Description: The OutlookMobileGCCRestrictionsEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable features within Outlook for iOS and Android that are not FedRAMP compliant for Office 365 US Government Community Cloud (GCC) customers.
Parameters.Name: OutlookMobileGCCRestrictionsEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OutlookPayEnabled parameter enables or disables Payments in Outlook in the Office 365 organization.
Parameters.Name: OutlookPayEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for OutlookTextPredictionDisabled.
Parameters.Name: OutlookTextPredictionDisabled

Parameters.Description: The PublicComputersDetectionEnabled parameter specifies whether Outlook on the web will detect when a user signs from a public or private computer or network, and then enforces the attachment handling settings from public networks. The default is $false. However, if you set this parameter to $true, Outlook on the web will determine if the user is signing in from a public computer, and all public attachment handling rules will be applied and enforced.
Parameters.Name: PublicComputersDetectionEnabled

Parameters.Description: The PublicFoldersEnabled parameter specifies how public folders are deployed in your organization.
Parameters.Name: PublicFoldersEnabled

Parameters.Description: The PublicFolderShowClientControl parameter enables or disables access to public folders in Microsoft Outlook.
Parameters.Name: PublicFolderShowClientControl

Parameters.Description: The ReadTrackingEnabled parameter specifies whether the tracking for read status for messages in an organization is enabled. The default value is $false.
Parameters.Name: ReadTrackingEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for RecallReadMessagesEnabled.
Parameters.Name: RecallReadMessagesEnabled

Parameters.Description: The RemotePublicFolderMailboxes parameter specifies the identities of the public folder objects (represented as mail user objects locally) corresponding to the public folder mailboxes created in the remote forest. The public folder values set here are used only if the public folder deployment is a remote deployment.
Parameters.Name: RemotePublicFolderMailboxes

Parameters.Description: The SendFromAliasEnabled parameter allows mailbox users to send messages using aliases (proxy addresses). It does this by disabling the rewriting of aliases to their primary SMTP address. This change is implemented in the Exchange Online service
Parameters.Name: SendFromAliasEnabled

Parameters.Description: No description available for SharedDomainEmailAddressFlowEnabled.
Parameters.Name: SharedDomainEmailAddressFlowEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ShortenEventScopeDefault parameter specifies whether calendar events start late or end early in the organization.
Parameters.Name: ShortenEventScopeDefault

Parameters.Description: The SiteMailboxCreationURL parameter specifies the URL that's used to create site mailboxes. Site mailboxes improve collaboration and user productivity by allowing access to both SharePoint documents and Exchange email in Outlook 2013 or later.
Parameters.Name: SiteMailboxCreationURL

Parameters.Description: The SmtpActionableMessagesEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable action buttons in email messages in Outlook on the web.
Parameters.Name: SmtpActionableMessagesEnabled

Parameters.Description: The TenantAdminNotificationForDelayedDelicensingEnabled parameter enables or disables weekly admin Service Health advisory notifications that are sent to admins.
Parameters.Name: TenantAdminNotificationForDelayedDelicensingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The VisibleMeetingUpdateProperties parameter specifies whether meeting message updates will be auto-processed on behalf of attendees. Auto-processed updates are applied to the attendee's calendar item, and then the meeting message is moved to the deleted items. The attendee never sees the update in their inbox, but their calendar is updated.
Parameters.Name: VisibleMeetingUpdateProperties

Parameters.Description: The WebPushNotificationsDisabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Web Push Notifications in Outlook on the Web. This feature provides web push notifications which appear on a user's desktop while the user is not using Outlook on the Web. This brings awareness of incoming messages while they are working elsewhere on their computer.
Parameters.Name: WebPushNotificationsDisabled

Parameters.Description: The WebSuggestedRepliesDisabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Suggested Replies in Outlook on the web. This feature provides suggested replies to emails so users can easily and quickly respond to messages.
Parameters.Name: WebSuggestedRepliesDisabled

Parameters.Description: The WorkspaceTenantEnabled parameter enables or disables workspace booking in the organization.
Parameters.Name: WorkspaceTenantEnabled

Parameters.Description: This parameter is available only in the cloud-based service.
Parameters.Name: RejectDirectSend

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.
Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword

Parameters.Name: CertificatePath

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity

Description: This resource configures the Exchange Online organization-wide settings.

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the organization relationship. The maximum length is 64 characters.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The ArchiveAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether the organization relationship has been configured to provide remote archive access.
Parameters.Name: ArchiveAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DeliveryReportEnabled parameter specifies whether Delivery Reports should be shared over the organization relationship.
Parameters.Name: DeliveryReportEnabled

Parameters.Description: The DomainNames parameter specifies the SMTP domains of the external organization. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: DomainNames

Parameters.Description: The Enabled parameter specifies whether to enable the organization relationship.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: The FreeBusyAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether the organization relationship should be used to retrieve free/busy information from the external organization.
Parameters.Name: FreeBusyAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The FreeBusyAccessLevel parameter specifies the maximum amount of detail returned to the requesting organization. Valid values are: None, AvailabilityOnly or LimitedDetails
Parameters.Name: FreeBusyAccessLevel

Parameters.Description: The FreeBusyAccessScope parameter specifies a mail-enabled security group in the internal organization that contains users whose free/busy information is accessible by an external organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: FreeBusyAccessScope

Parameters.Description: The MailboxMoveEnabled parameter specifies whether the organization relationship enables moving mailboxes to or from the external organization.
Parameters.Name: MailboxMoveEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailboxMoveCapability parameter is used in cross-tenant mailbox migrations.
Parameters.Name: MailboxMoveCapability

Parameters.Description: The MailboxMovePublishedScopes parameter is used in cross-tenant mailbox migrations to specify the mail-enabled security groups whose members are allowed to migrate.
Parameters.Name: MailboxMovePublishedScopes

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsAccessEnabled parameter specifies whether MailTips for users in this organization are returned over this organization relationship.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsAccessEnabled

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsAccessLevel parameter specifies the level of MailTips data externally shared over this organization relationship. This parameter can have the following values: All, Limited, None
Parameters.Name: MailTipsAccessLevel

Parameters.Description: The MailTipsAccessScope parameter specifies a mail-enabled security group in the internal organization that contains users whose free/busy information is accessible by an external organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.
Parameters.Name: MailTipsAccessScope

Parameters.Description: The OAuthApplicationId is used in cross-tenant mailbox migrations to specify the application ID of the mailbox migration app that you consented to.
Parameters.Name: OauthApplicationId

Parameters.Description: The OrganizationContact parameter specifies the email address that can be used to contact the external organization (for example, administrator@fourthcoffee.com).
Parameters.Name: OrganizationContact

Parameters.Description: The PhotosEnabled parameter specifies whether photos for users in the internal organization are returned over the organization relationship.
Parameters.Name: PhotosEnabled

Parameters.Description: The TargetApplicationUri parameter specifies the target Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the external organization. The TargetApplicationUri parameter is specified by Exchange when requesting a delegated token to retrieve free and busy information, for example, mail.contoso.com.
Parameters.Name: TargetApplicationUri

Parameters.Description: The TargetAutodiscoverEpr parameter specifies the Autodiscover URL of Exchange Web Services for the external organization. Exchange uses Autodiscover to automatically detect the correct Exchangeserver endpoint to use for external requests.
Parameters.Name: TargetAutodiscoverEpr

Parameters.Description: The TargetOwaURL parameter specifies the Outlook on the web (formerly Outlook Web App) URL of the external organization that's defined in the organization relationship. It is used for Outlook on the web redirection in a cross-premise Exchange scenario. Configuring this attribute enables users in the organization to use their current Outlook on the web URL to access Outlook on the web in the external organization.
Parameters.Name: TargetOwaURL

Parameters.Description: The TargetSharingEpr parameter specifies the URL of the target Exchange Web Services for the external organization.
Parameters.Name: TargetSharingEpr

Parameters.Description: Specify if the OrganizationRelationship should exist or not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Exchange Global Admin
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.
Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.
Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Description: Username can be made up to anything but password will be used for CertificatePassword
Parameters.Name: CertificatePassword

Parameters.Description: Path to certificate used in service principal usually a PFX file.
Parameters.Name: CertificatePath

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.

Description: This resource configures the Organization Relationship in Exchange Online.

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the outbound connector that you want to modify.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether connector is enabled.
Parameters.Name: Enabled

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether connector should use MXRecords for target resolution.
Parameters.Name: UseMXRecord

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorSource parameter specifies how the connector is created. DO NOT CHANGE THIS!
Parameters.Name: ConnectorSource

Parameters.Description: The ConnectorType parameter specifies a category for the domains that are serviced by the connector.
Parameters.Name: ConnectorType

Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomains parameter specifies the domain that the Outbound connector routes mail to. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: RecipientDomains

Parameters.Description: The SmartHosts parameter specifies the smart hosts the Outbound connector uses to route mail. This parameter is required if you set the UseMxRecord parameter to $false and must be specified on the same command line.
Parameters.Name: SmartHosts

Parameters.Description: The TlsDomain parameter specifies the domain name that the Outbound connector uses to verify the FQDN of the target certificate when establishing a TLS secured connection. This parameter is only used if the TlsSettings parameter is set to DomainValidation. Valid input for the TlsDomain parameter is an SMTP domain. You can use a wildcard character to specify all subdomains of a specified domain, as shown in the following example: *.contoso.com. However, you can't embed a wildcard character, as shown in the following example: domain.*.contoso.com
Parameters.Name: TlsDomain

Parameters.Description: The TlsSettings parameter specifies the TLS authentication level that's used for outbound TLS connections established by this Outbound connector.
Parameters.Name: TlsSettings

Parameters.Description: The IsTransportRuleScoped parameter specifies whether the Outbound connector is associated with a transport rule (also known as a mail flow rule).
Parameters.Name: IsTransportRuleScoped

Parameters.Description: The RouteAllMessagesViaOnPremises parameter specifies that all messages serviced by this connector are first routed through the on-premises messaging system (Centralized mailrouting).
Parameters.Name: RouteAllMessagesViaOnPremises

Parameters.Description: The CloudServicesMailEnabled parameter specifies whether the connector is used for hybrid mail flow between an on-premises Exchange environment and Microsoft Office 365. Specifically, this parameter controls how certain internal X-MS-Exchange-Organization-* message headers are handled in messages that are sent between accepted domains in the on-premises and cloud organizations. These headers are collectively known as cross-premises headers. DO NOT USE MANUALLY!
Parameters.Name: CloudServicesMailEnabled

Parameters.Description: The AllAcceptedDomains parameter specifies whether the Outbound connector is used in hybrid organizations where message recipients are in accepted domains of the cloud-based organization.

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter specifies the room mailbox that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the room.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The display name of the place.
Parameters.Name: DisplayName

Parameters.Description: The AudioDeviceName parameter specifies the name of the audio device in the room. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: AudioDeviceName

Parameters.Description: The Building parameter specifies the building name or building number that the room is in. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Building

Parameters.Description: The Capacity parameter specifies the capacity of the room. A valid value is an integer.
Parameters.Name: Capacity

Parameters.Description: The City parameter specifies the room's city. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: City

Parameters.Description: The CountryOrRegion parameter specifies the room's country or region. A valid value is a valid ISO 3166-1 two-letter country code (for example, AU for Australia) or the corresponding friendly name for the country (which might be different from the official ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency short name).
Parameters.Name: CountryOrRegion

Parameters.Description: N/A
Parameters.Name: Desks

Parameters.Description: The DisplayDeviceName parameter specifies the name of the display device in the room. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: DisplayDeviceName

Parameters.Name: Floor

Parameters.Name: FloorLabel

Parameters.Name: GeoCoordinates

Parameters.Description: The IsWheelChairAccessible parameter specifies whether the room is wheelchair accessible.
Parameters.Name: IsWheelChairAccessible

Parameters.Description: The Label parameter specifies a descriptive label for the room (for example, a number or name). If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Label

Parameters.Description: The MTREnabled parameter identifies the room as configured with a Microsoft Teams room system. You can add Teams room systems as audio sources in Teams meetings that involve the room.
Parameters.Name: MTREnabled

Parameters.Description: The ParentId parameter specifies the ID of a Place in the parent location hierarchy in Microsoft Places.
Parameters.Name: ParentId

Parameters.Description: The ParentType parameter specifies the parent type of the ParentId in Microsoft Places. Valid values are: Floor, Section
Parameters.Name: ParentType

Parameters.Description: The Phone parameter specifies the room's telephone number.
Parameters.Name: Phone

Parameters.Description: The PostalCode parameter specifies the room's postal code.
Parameters.Name: PostalCode

Parameters.Description: The State parameter specifies the room's state or province.
Parameters.Name: State

Parameters.Description: The Street parameter specifies the room's physical address.
Parameters.Name: Street

Parameters.Description: The Tags parameter specifies additional features of the room (for example, details like the type of view or furniture type).
Parameters.Name: Tags

Parameters.Description: The VideoDeviceName parameter specifies the name of the video device in the room. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: VideoDeviceName
