Parameters.Description: Indicates the network settings of the Cloud PC during a cross-region disaster recovery operation.
Parameters.Name: DisasterRecoveryNetworkSetting

Parameters.Description: Indicates the type of disaster recovery to perform when a disaster occurs on the user's Cloud PC. The possible values are: notConfigured, crossRegion, premium, unknownFutureValue. The default value is notConfigured.
Parameters.Name: DisasterRecoveryType

Parameters.Description: Indicates whether Windows 365 maintain the cross-region disaster recovery function generated restore points. If true, the Windows 365 stored restore points false indicates that Windows 365 doesn't generate or keep the restore point from the original Cloud PC. If a disaster occurs, the new Cloud PC can only be provisioned using the initial image. This limitation can result in the loss of some user data on the original Cloud PC. The default value is false.
Parameters.Name: MaintainCrossRegionRestorePointEnabled

Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the client allows the end user to initiate a disaster recovery activation. True indicates that the client includes the option for the end user to activate Backup Cloud PC. When false, the end user doesn't have the option to activate disaster recovery. The default value is false. Currently, only premium disaster recovery is supported.
Parameters.Name: UserInitiatedDisasterRecoveryAllowed

Description: Intune User Settings Policy for Windows365

Parameters.Description: Indicates the display name of the virtual network that the new Cloud PC joins.  Only applicable for the '#microsoft.graph.cloudPcDisasterRecoveryAzureConnectionSetting' odata type.
Parameters.Name: OnPremisesConnectionId

Parameters.Description: Indicates the logic geographic group this region belongs to. Multiple regions can belong to one region group. When a region group is configured for disaster recovery, the new Cloud PC is assigned to one of the regions within the group based on resource availability. For example, the europeUnion region group contains the North Europe and West Europe regions.  Only applicable for the '#microsoft.graph.cloudPcDisasterRecoveryMicrosoftHostedNetworkSetting' odata type. Possible values are: default, australia, canada, usCentral, usEast, usWest, france, germany, europeUnion, unitedKingdom, japan, asia, india, southAmerica, euap, usGovernment, usGovernmentDOD, unknownFutureValue, norway, switzerland, southKorea, middleEast, mexico, australasia, europe. Use the Prefer: include-unknown-enum-members request header to get the following values in this evolvable enum: norway, switzerland, southKorea, middleEast, mexico, australasia, europe.
Parameters.Name: RegionGroup

Parameters.Description: Indicates the Azure region that the new Cloud PC is assigned to. The Windows 365 service creates and manages the underlying virtual network. Only applicable for the '#microsoft.graph.cloudPcDisasterRecoveryMicrosoftHostedNetworkSetting' odata type.
Parameters.Name: RegionName

Parameters.Description: The type of the entity.
Parameters.Name: odataType

Description: Intune User Settings Policy for Windows365

Parameters.Description: If true, doesn't prompt the user to restart the Cloud PC. If false, prompts the user to restart Cloud PC. The default value is false.
Parameters.Name: RestartPromptsDisabled

Description: Intune User Settings Policy for Windows365

Parameters.Description: The time interval in hours to take snapshots (restore points) of a Cloud PC automatically. Possible values are: default, fourHours, sixHours, twelveHours, sixteenHours, twentyFourHours. The default value is default that indicates that the time interval for automatic capturing of restore point snapshots is set to 12 hours.
Parameters.Name: FrequencyType

Parameters.Description: If true, the user has the ability to use snapshots to restore Cloud PCs. If false, non-admin users can't use snapshots to restore the Cloud PC.
Parameters.Name: UserRestoreEnabled

Description: Intune User Settings Policy for Windows365

Parameters.Description: Defines whether the user's Cloud PC enables cross-region disaster recovery and specifies the network for the disaster recovery.
Parameters.Name: CrossRegionDisasterRecoverySetting

Parameters.Description: The setting name displayed in the user interface.
Parameters.Name: DisplayName

Parameters.Description: Indicates whether the local admin option is enabled. Default value is false. To enable the local admin option, change the setting to true. If the local admin option is enabled, the end user can be an admin of the Cloud PC device.
Parameters.Name: LocalAdminEnabled

Parameters.Description: Defines the setting of the Cloud PC notification prompts for the Cloud PC user.
Parameters.Name: NotificationSetting

Parameters.Description: Indicates whether an end user is allowed to reset their Cloud PC. When true, the user is allowed to reset their Cloud PC. When false, end-user initiated reset isn't allowed. The default value is false.
Parameters.Name: ResetEnabled

Parameters.Description: Defines how frequently a restore point is created (that is, a snapshot is taken) for users' provisioned Cloud PCs (default is 12 hours), and whether the user is allowed to restore their own Cloud PCs to a backup made at a specific point in time.
Parameters.Name: RestorePointSetting

Parameters.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.
Parameters.Name: Id

Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.
Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance

Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to use the anti-spoofing features for facial recognition on devices which support it. If set to disabled, anti-spoofing features are not allowed. If set to Not Configured, the user can choose whether they want to use anti-spoofing. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.
Parameters.Name: EnhancedBiometricsState

Parameters.Description: Setting to configure Enhanced sign-in security. Default is Not Configured
Parameters.Name: EnhancedSignInSecurity

Parameters.Description: Controls the period of time (in days) that a PIN can be used before the system requires the user to change it. This must be set between 0 and 730, inclusive. If set to 0, the user's PIN will never expire
Parameters.Name: PinExpirationInDays

Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to use lowercase letters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  Allowed permits the use of lowercase letter(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, lowercase letters will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.
Parameters.Name: PinLowercaseCharactersUsage

Parameters.Description: Controls the maximum number of characters allowed for the Windows Hello for Business PIN. This value must be between 4 and 127, inclusive. This value must be greater than or equal to the value set for the minimum PIN.
Parameters.Name: PinMaximumLength

Parameters.Description: Controls the minimum number of characters required for the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  This value must be between 4 and 127, inclusive, and less than or equal to the value set for the maximum PIN.
Parameters.Name: PinMinimumLength

Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to prevent users from using past PINs. This must be set between 0 and 50, inclusive, and the current PIN of the user is included in that count. If set to 0, previous PINs are not stored. PIN history is not preserved through a PIN reset.
Parameters.Name: PinPreviousBlockCount

Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to use special characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  Allowed permits the use of special character(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, special character(s) will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.
Parameters.Name: PinSpecialCharactersUsage

Parameters.Description: Controls the ability to use uppercase letters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  Allowed permits the use of uppercase letter(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, uppercase letters will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.
Parameters.Name: PinUppercaseCharactersUsage

Parameters.Description: Controls the use of Remote Windows Hello for Business. Remote Windows Hello for Business provides the ability for a portable, registered device to be usable as a companion for desktop authentication. The desktop must be Azure AD joined and the companion device must have a Windows Hello for Business PIN.
Parameters.Name: RemotePassportEnabled

Parameters.Description: Controls whether to require a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) for provisioning Windows Hello for Business. A TPM provides an additional security benefit in that data stored on it cannot be used on other devices. If set to False, all devices can provision Windows Hello for Business even if there is not a usable TPM.
Parameters.Name: SecurityDeviceRequired

Parameters.Description: Security key for Sign In provides the capacity for remotely turning ON/OFF Windows Hello Sercurity Keyl Not configured will honor configurations done on the clinet. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.
Parameters.Name: SecurityKeyForSignIn

Parameters.Description: Controls whether to allow the device to be configured for Windows Hello for Business. If set to disabled, the user cannot provision Windows Hello for Business except on Azure Active Directory joined mobile phones if otherwise required. If set to Not Configured, Intune will not override client defaults. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.
Parameters.Name: State

Parameters.Description: Controls the use of biometric gestures, such as face and fingerprint, as an alternative to the Windows Hello for Business PIN.  If set to False, biometric gestures are not allowed. Users must still configure a PIN as a backup in case of failures.
Parameters.Name: UnlockWithBiometricsEnabled

Parameters.Description: This is the comma-separated list of internal proxy servers. For example, '157.54.14.28, 157.54.11.118, 10.202.14.167, 157.53.14.163, 157.69.210.59'. These proxies have been configured by the admin to connect to specific resources on the Internet. They are considered to be enterprise network locations. The proxies are only leveraged in configuring the EnterpriseProxiedDomains policy to force traffic to the matched domains through these proxies
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseInternalProxyServers

Parameters.Description: Sets the enterprise IP ranges that define the computers in the enterprise network. Data that comes from those computers will be considered part of the enterprise and protected. These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRanges

Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list and not to use heuristics to attempt to find other subnets. Default is false
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseIPRangesAreAuthoritative

Parameters.Description: This is the list of domains that comprise the boundaries of the enterprise. Data from one of these domains that is sent to a device will be considered enterprise data and protected These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseNetworkDomainNames

Parameters.Description: List of enterprise domains to be protected
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProtectedDomainNames

Parameters.Description: Contains a list of Enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud that need to be protected. Connections to these resources are considered enterprise data. If a proxy is paired with a cloud resource, traffic to the cloud resource will be routed through the enterprise network via the denoted proxy server (on Port 80). A proxy server used for this purpose must also be configured using the EnterpriseInternalProxyServers policy
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxiedDomains

Parameters.Description: This is a list of proxy servers. Any server not on this list is considered non-enterprise
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServers

Parameters.Description: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list of proxies and not try to detect other work proxies. Default is false
Parameters.Name: EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative

Parameters.Description: Exempt applications can also access enterprise data, but the data handled by those applications are not protected. This is because some critical enterprise applications may have compatibility problems with encrypted data.
Parameters.Name: ExemptApps

Parameters.Description: Determines whether overlays are added to icons for WIP protected files in Explorer and enterprise only app tiles in the Start menu. Starting in Windows 10, version 1703 this setting also configures the visibility of the WIP icon in the title bar of a WIP-protected app
Parameters.Name: IconsVisible

Parameters.Description: This switch is for the Windows Search Indexer, to allow or disallow indexing of items
Parameters.Name: IndexingEncryptedStoresOrItemsBlocked

Parameters.Description: List of domain names that can used for work or personal resource
Parameters.Name: NeutralDomainResources

Parameters.Description: Protected applications can access enterprise data and the data handled by those applications are protected with encryption
Parameters.Name: ProtectedApps

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether the protection under lock feature (also known as encrypt under pin) should be configured
Parameters.Name: ProtectionUnderLockConfigRequired

Parameters.Description: This policy controls whether to revoke the WIP keys when a device unenrolls from the management service. If set to 1 (Don't revoke keys), the keys will not be revoked and the user will continue to have access to protected files after unenrollment. If the keys are not revoked, there will be no revoked file cleanup subsequently.
Parameters.Name: RevokeOnUnenrollDisabled

Parameters.Description: TemplateID GUID to use for RMS encryption. The RMS template allows the IT admin to configure the details about who has access to RMS-protected file and how long they have access
Parameters.Name: RightsManagementServicesTemplateId

Parameters.Description: Specifies a list of file extensions, so that files with these extensions are encrypted when copying from an SMB share within the corporate boundary
Parameters.Name: SmbAutoEncryptedFileExtensions

Description:
Intune Windows Update For Business Feature Update Profile for Windows10

## RolloutSettings

The RolloutSettings for this resource have the following constraints and notes:

* When creating a policy:
  * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must be at least today.
    * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
  * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
    * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When updating a policy:
  * If only a start date is specified, then the start date must either be the date from the current configuration or the current date (or later).
    * If the desired state date is before the current date, it will be adjusted to the current date.
  * If a start and end date is specified, the start date must be the current date + 2 days, and the end date must be at least one day after the start date.
    * If the start date is before the current date + 2 days, it will be adjusted to this date.
* When testing a policy:
  * If the policy is missing and the start and end date are before the current date, it will return true.
  * If the start date is different but before the current start date or time, it will return true.

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, allows eligible Windows 10 devices to upgrade to Windows 11. When FALSE, implies the device stays on the existing operating system. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: AllowWindows11Upgrade

Parameters.Description: The Automatic Update Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, NotifyDownload, AutoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, AutoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, WindowsDefault. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, notifyDownload, autoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, autoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, windowsDefault.
Parameters.Name: AutomaticUpdateMode

Parameters.Description: Specify the method by which the auto-restart required notification is dismissed. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Automatic, User. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, automatic, user, unknownFutureValue.
Parameters.Name: AutoRestartNotificationDismissal

Parameters.Description: Determines which branch devices will receive their updates from. Possible values are: UserDefined, All, BusinessReadyOnly, WindowsInsiderBuildFast, WindowsInsiderBuildSlow, WindowsInsiderBuildRelease. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, all, businessReadyOnly, windowsInsiderBuildFast, windowsInsiderBuildSlow, windowsInsiderBuildRelease.
Parameters.Name: BusinessReadyUpdatesOnly

Parameters.Description: Number of days before feature updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: DeadlineForFeatureUpdatesInDays

Parameters.Description: Number of days before quality updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: DeadlineForQualityUpdatesInDays

Parameters.Description: Number of days after deadline until restarts occur automatically with valid range from 0 to 7 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: DeadlineGracePeriodInDays

Parameters.Description: The Delivery Optimization Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, HttpOnly, HttpWithPeeringNat, HttpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, HttpWithInternetPeering, SimpleDownload, BypassMode. UserDefined allows the user to set. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, httpOnly, httpWithPeeringNat, httpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, httpWithInternetPeering, simpleDownload, bypassMode.
Parameters.Name: DeliveryOptimizationMode

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, excludes Windows update Drivers. When FALSE, does not exclude Windows update Drivers. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: DriversExcluded

Parameters.Description: Deadline in days before automatically scheduling and executing a pending restart outside of active hours, with valid range from 2 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartDeadlineInDays

Parameters.Description: Number of days a user can snooze Engaged Restart reminder notifications with valid range from 1 to 3 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartSnoozeScheduleInDays

Parameters.Description: Number of days before transitioning from Auto Restarts scheduled outside of active hours to Engaged Restart, which requires the user to schedule, with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: EngagedRestartTransitionScheduleInDays

Parameters.Description: Defer Feature Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving feature updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Feature Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.s
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPaused

Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime

Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesPauseStartDate

Parameters.Description: The Feature Updates Rollback Start datetime.This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Feature update for the ring.Returned by default.Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime

Parameters.Description: The number of days after a Feature Update for which a rollback is valid with valid range from 2 to 60 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: FeatureUpdatesRollbackWindowInDays

Parameters.Description: The Installation Schedule. Possible values are: ActiveHoursStart, ActiveHoursEnd, ScheduledInstallDay, ScheduledInstallTime. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: InstallationSchedule

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, allows Microsoft Update Service. When FALSE, does not allow Microsoft Update Service. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: MicrosoftUpdateServiceAllowed

Parameters.Description: When TRUE the device should wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. When FALSE the device should not wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: PostponeRebootUntilAfterDeadline

Parameters.Description: The Pre-Release Features. Possible values are: UserDefined, SettingsOnly, SettingsAndExperimentations, NotAllowed. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, settingsOnly, settingsAndExperimentations, notAllowed.
Parameters.Name: PrereleaseFeatures

Parameters.Description: Defer Quality Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving quality updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Quality Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPaused

Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime

Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.
Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesPauseStartDate

Parameters.Description: The Quality Updates Rollback Start datetime. This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Quality update for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: QualityUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime

Parameters.Description: Specify the period for auto-restart imminent warning notifications. Supported values: 15, 30 or 60 (minutes). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: ScheduleImminentRestartWarningInMinutes

Parameters.Description: Specify the period for auto-restart warning reminder notifications. Supported values: 2, 4, 8, 12 or 24 (hours). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: ScheduleRestartWarningInHours

Parameters.Description: When TRUE, skips all checks before restart: Battery level = 40%, User presence, Display Needed, Presentation mode, Full screen mode, phone call state, game mode etc. When FALSE, does not skip all checks before restart. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.
Parameters.Name: SkipChecksBeforeRestart

Parameters.Description: Specifies what Windows Update notifications users see. Possible values are: NotConfigured, DefaultNotifications, RestartWarningsOnly, DisableAllNotifications. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, defaultNotifications, restartWarningsOnly, disableAllNotifications, unknownFutureValue.
Parameters.Name: UpdateNotificationLevel

Parameters.Description: Schedule the update installation on the weeks of the month. Possible values are: UserDefined, FirstWeek, SecondWeek, ThirdWeek, FourthWeek, EveryWeek. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, firstWeek, secondWeek, thirdWeek, fourthWeek, everyWeek, unknownFutureValue.
Parameters.Name: UpdateWeeks

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to enable end user's access to pause software updates. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.
Parameters.Name: UserPauseAccess

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether to disable user's access to scan Windows Update. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.
Parameters.Name: UserWindowsUpdateScanAccess

Description:
This resource configures the tenant settings so that the tenant runs in a fully
configurable mode. Usually tenants are created in a mode, that limits options to
customize the tenant. This setting is reflected by the Organization Config proptery
`IsDehydrated: True`.
Running this resource will set the property to false.

Currently there is no setting available to undo Enable-OrganizationCustomization.

Description: This resource configures an Auto Sensitivity Label Rule in Purview.

Parameters.Description: Name of the Rule.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: Name of the associated Policy.
Parameters.Name: Policy

Parameters.Description: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None
Parameters.Name: AccessScope

Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.
Parameters.Name: ContentContainsSensitiveInformation

Parameters.Description: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ContentExtensionMatchesWords

Parameters.Description: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the auto-labeling policy rule is enabled or disabled.
Parameters.Name: Disabled

Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.
Parameters.Name: DocumentIsPasswordProtected

Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.
Parameters.Name: DocumentIsUnsupported

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAccessScopeAccessScope parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule isn't applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAccessScope

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFrom parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFrom

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent by group members. You identify the group members by their email addresses. You can enter multiple values separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderIPRanges

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentToMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: FromAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: FromAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: HeaderMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete. You can use this condition to create rules that work together to identify and process messages where the content couldn't be fully scanned.
Parameters.Name: ProcessingLimitExceeded

Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule. Valid values are: None, Low, Medium, High
Parameters.Name: ReportSeverityLevel

Parameters.Description: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule. Valid values are: Ignore, RetryThenBlock, *blank*
Parameters.Name: RuleErrorAction

Parameters.Description: The SenderDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.
Parameters.Name: SenderDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The SenderIpRanges parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.
Parameters.Name: SenderIPRanges

Parameters.Description: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.
Parameters.Name: SentTo

Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address.
Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: SubjectMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The IncludeUserAppContent parameter specifies that you want to search the cloud-based storage location for users who don't have a regular Office 365 user account in your organization. Parameters.Name: AllowPassbookWhileLocked

Parameters.Name: AllowVideoConferencing

Parameters.Description: The AllowVoiceAssistant parameter specifies whether to allow using the voice assistant on devices.
Parameters.Name: AllowVoiceAssistant

Parameters.Description: The AllowVoiceDialing parameter specifies whether to allow voice-activated telephone dialing.
Parameters.Name: AllowVoiceDialing

Parameters.Description: The AntiVirusSignatureStatus parameter specifies the antivirus signature status.
Parameters.Name: AntiVirusSignatureStatus

Parameters.Description: The AntiVirusStatus parameter specifies the antivirus status.
Parameters.Name: AntiVirusStatus

Parameters.Description: The AppsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of apps that are allowed on devices.
Parameters.Name: AppsRating

Parameters.Description: The AutoUpdateStatus parameter specifies the update settings for devices.
Parameters.Name: AutoUpdateStatus

Parameters.Description: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.
Parameters.Name: BluetoothEnabled

Parameters.Description: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.
Parameters.Name: CameraEnabled

Parameters.Description: The EmailAddress parameter specifies the email address.
Parameters.Name: EmailAddress

Parameters.Description: The EnableRemovableStorage parameter specifies whether removable storage can be used by devices.
Parameters.Name: EnableRemovableStorage

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeActiveSyncHost parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync host.
Parameters.Name: ExchangeActiveSyncHost

Parameters.Description: The FirewallStatus parameter specifies the acceptable firewall status values on devices.
Parameters.Name: FirewallStatus

Parameters.Description: The ForceAppStorePassword parameter specifies whether to require a password to use the app store on devices.
Parameters.Name: ForceAppStorePassword

Parameters.Description: The ForceEncryptedBackup parameter specifies whether to force encrypted backups for devices.
Parameters.Name: ForceEncryptedBackup

Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe parameter specifies the number of incorrect password attempts that cause devices to be automatically wiped.
Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe

Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordGracePeriod parameter specifies the length of time users are allowed to reset expired passwords on devices.
Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordGracePeriod

Parameters.Description: The MoviesRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of movies that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.
Parameters.Name: MoviesRating

Parameters.Description: The PasswordComplexity parameter specifies the password complexity.
Parameters.Name: PasswordComplexity

Parameters.Description: The PasswordExpirationDays parameter specifies the number of days that the same password can be used on devices before users are required to change their passwords.
Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays

Parameters.Description: The PasswordHistoryCount parameter specifies the minimum number of unique new passwords that are required on devices before an old password can be reused.
Parameters.Name: PasswordHistoryCount

Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinComplexChars parameter specifies the minimum number of complex characters that are required for device passwords. A complex character isn't a letter.
Parameters.Name: PasswordMinComplexChars

Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinimumLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters that are required for device passwords.
Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength

Parameters.Description: The PasswordQuality parameter specifies the minimum password quality rating that's required for device passwords. Password quality is a numeric scale that indicates the security and complexity of the password. A higher quality value indicates a more secure password.
Parameters.Name: PasswordQuality

Parameters.Description: The PasswordRequired parameter specifies whether a password is required to access devices.
Parameters.Name: PasswordRequired

Parameters.Description: The PasswordTimeout parameter specifies the length of time that devices can be inactive before a password is required to reactivate them.
Parameters.Name: PasswordTimeout

Parameters.Description: The PhoneMemoryEncrypted parameter specifies whether to encrypt the memory on devices.
Parameters.Name: PhoneMemoryEncrypted

Parameters.Description: The RegionRatings parameter specifies the rating system (country/region) to use for movie and television ratings with the MoviesRating and TVShowsRating parameters.
Parameters.Name: RegionRatings

Parameters.Description: The RequireEmailProfile parameter specifies whether an email profile is required on devices.
Parameters.Name: RequireEmailProfile

Parameters.Description: The SmartScreenEnabled parameter specifies whether to requireWindows SmartScreen on devices.
Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SystemSecurityTLS parameter specifies whether TLS encryption is used on devices.
Parameters.Name: SystemSecurityTLS

Parameters.Description: The TVShowsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of television shows that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.
Parameters.Name: TVShowsRating

Parameters.Description: The UserAccountControlStatus parameter specifies how User Account Control messages are presented on devices.
Parameters.Name: UserAccountControlStatus

Parameters.Description: The WLANEnabled parameter specifies whether Wi-Fi is enabled devices.
Parameters.Name: WLANEnabled

Parameters.Description: The WorkFoldersSyncUrl parameter specifies the URL that's used to synchronize company data on devices.
Parameters.Name: WorkFoldersSyncUrl

Parameters.Name: ForceEncryptedBackup

Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe parameter specifies the number of incorrect password attempts that cause devices to be automatically wiped.
Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe

Parameters.Description: The MaxPasswordGracePeriod parameter specifies the length of time users are allowed to reset expired passwords on devices.
Parameters.Name: MaxPasswordGracePeriod

Parameters.Description: The MoviesRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of movies that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.
Parameters.Name: MoviesRating

Parameters.Description: The PasswordComplexity parameter specifies the password complexity.
Parameters.Name: PasswordComplexity

Parameters.Description: The PasswordExpirationDays parameter specifies the number of days that the same password can be used on devices before users are required to change their passwords.
Parameters.Name: PasswordExpirationDays

Parameters.Description: The PasswordHistoryCount parameter specifies the minimum number of unique new passwords that are required on devices before an old password can be reused.
Parameters.Name: PasswordHistoryCount

Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinComplexChars parameter specifies the minimum number of complex characters that are required for device passwords. A complex character isn't a letter.
Parameters.Name: PasswordMinComplexChars

Parameters.Description: The PasswordMinimumLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters that are required for device passwords.
Parameters.Name: PasswordMinimumLength

Parameters.Description: The PasswordQuality parameter specifies the minimum password quality rating that's required for device passwords. Password quality is a numeric scale that indicates the security and complexity of the password. A higher quality value indicates a more secure password.
Parameters.Name: PasswordQuality

Parameters.Description: The PasswordRequired parameter specifies whether a password is required to access devices.
Parameters.Name: PasswordRequired

Parameters.Description: The PasswordTimeout parameter specifies the length of time that devices can be inactive before a password is required to reactivate them.
Parameters.Name: PasswordTimeout

Parameters.Description: The PhoneMemoryEncrypted parameter specifies whether to encrypt the memory on devices.
Parameters.Name: PhoneMemoryEncrypted

Parameters.Description: The RegionRatings parameter specifies the rating system (country/region) to use for movie and television ratings with the MoviesRating and TVShowsRating parameters.
Parameters.Name: RegionRatings

Parameters.Description: The RequireEmailProfile parameter specifies whether an email profile is required on devices.
Parameters.Name: RequireEmailProfile

Parameters.Description: The SmartScreenEnabled parameter specifies whether to requireWindows SmartScreen on devices.
Parameters.Name: SmartScreenEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SystemSecurityTLS parameter specifies whether TLS encryption is used on devices.
Parameters.Name: SystemSecurityTLS

Parameters.Description: The TVShowsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of television shows that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.
Parameters.Name: TVShowsRating

Parameters.Description: The UserAccountControlStatus parameter specifies how User Account Control messages are presented on devices.
Parameters.Name: UserAccountControlStatus

Parameters.Description: The WLANEnabled parameter specifies whether Wi-Fi is enabled devices.
Parameters.Name: WLANEnabled

Parameters.Description: The WorkFoldersSyncUrl parameter specifies the URL that's used to synchronize company data on devices.
Parameters.Name: WorkFoldersSyncUrl

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Name: EndpointDlpLocation

Parameters.Description: The EndpointDlpLocationException parameter specifies the user accounts to exclude from Endpoint DLP when you use the value All for the EndpointDlpLocation parameter. You identify the account by name or email address.
Parameters.Name: EndpointDlpLocationException

Parameters.Description: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders.
Parameters.Name: OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation

Parameters.Description: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to exclude from the DLP policy if you use the value All for the OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException

Parameters.Description: The PowerBIDlpLocation parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to include in the DLP policy. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted. You can use the value All to include all supported workspaces.
Parameters.Name: PowerBIDlpLocation

Parameters.Description: The PowerBIDlpLocationException parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the PowerBIDlpLocation parameter. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted.
Parameters.Name: PowerBIDlpLocationException

Parameters.Description: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all connected apps.
Parameters.Name: ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation

Parameters.Description: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies Exchange Online mailboxes to include in the DLP policy. You can only use the value All for this parameter to include all mailboxes.

Parameters.Description: Exchange members to exclude.

Parameters.Description: The Mode parameter specifies the action and notification level of the DLP policy. Valid values are: Enable, TestWithNotifications, TestWithoutNotifications, Disable and PendingDeletion.
Parameters.Name: Mode

Parameters.Description: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.
Parameters.Name: OneDriveLocation

Parameters.Description: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

Description: This resource configures a Data Loss Prevention Compliance Policy in Purview.

Parameters.Description: Name of the Rule.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: Name of the associated DLP Compliance Policy.
Parameters.Name: Policy

Parameters.Description: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope.
Parameters.Name: AccessScope

Parameters.Description: The BlockAccess parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that blocks access to the source item when the conditions of the rule are met. $true: Blocks further access to the source item that matched the rule. The owner, author, and site owner can still access the item. $false: Allows access to the source item that matched the rule. This is the default value.
Parameters.Name: BlockAccess

Parameters.Description: The BlockAccessScope parameter specifies the scope of the block access action.
Parameters.Name: BlockAccessScope

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The AdvancedRule parameter uses complex rule syntax that supports multiple AND, OR, and NOT operators and nested groups
Parameters.Name: AdvancedRule

Parameters.Description: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.
Parameters.Name: ContentContainsSensitiveInformation

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation

Parameters.Description: The ContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified property.
Parameters.Name: ContentPropertyContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the DLP rule is disabled.
Parameters.Name: Disabled

Parameters.Description: The GenerateAlert parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.
Parameters.Name: GenerateAlert

Parameters.Description: The GenerateIncidentReport parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that sends an incident report to the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.
Parameters.Name: GenerateIncidentReport

Parameters.Description: The IncidentReportContent parameter specifies the content to include in the report when you use the GenerateIncidentReport parameter.
Parameters.Name: IncidentReportContent

Parameters.Description: The NotifyAllowOverride parameter specifies the notification override options when the conditions of the rule are met.
Parameters.Name: NotifyAllowOverride

Parameters.Description: The NotifyEmailCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the email notification message that's sent to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met.
Parameters.Name: NotifyEmailCustomText

Parameters.Description: The NotifyPolicyTipCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the Policy Tip notification message that's shown to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met. The maximum length is 256 characters. HTML tags and tokens (variables) aren't supported.
Parameters.Name: NotifyPolicyTipCustomText

Parameters.Description: The NotifyUser parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.
Parameters.Name: NotifyUser

Parameters.Description: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule.
Parameters.Name: ReportSeverityLevel

Parameters.Description: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule.
Parameters.Name: RuleErrorAction

Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.

Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.
Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The RemoveRMSTemplate parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that removes Office 365 Message Encryption from messages and their attachments.
Parameters.Name: RemoveRMSTemplate

Parameters.Description: The StopPolicyProcessing parameter specifies an action that stops processing more DLP policy rules.
Parameters.Name: StopPolicyProcessing

Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.
Parameters.Name: DocumentIsUnsupported

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported

Parameters.Description: The SenderOverride parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.
Parameters.Name: HasSenderOverride

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfHasSenderOverride parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfHasSenderOverride

Parameters.Description: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.
Parameters.Name: ProcessingLimitExceeded

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded

Parameters.Description: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.
Parameters.Name: DocumentIsPasswordProtected

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected

Parameters.Description: The MessageTypeMatches parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for types of SMIME message patterns.
Parameters.Name: MessageTypeMatches

Parameters.Description: The FromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.
Parameters.Name: FromScope

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromScope

Parameters.Description: The SubjectContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: SubjectContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: SubjectMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The SubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.
Parameters.Name: SubjectOrBodyContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.
Parameters.Name: SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for character set names in messages. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ContentCharacterSetContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The DocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: DocumentNameMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The DocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.
Parameters.Name: DocumentNameMatchesWords

Parameters.Description: he ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for character set names in messages.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.
Parameters.Name: FromAddressContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: FromAddressMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages of the specified type.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches

Parameters.Description: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.
Parameters.Name: RecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderDomainIs

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSenderIPRanges

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients in messages. You identify the recipients by email address.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSentTo

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns

Parameters.Description: The DocumentContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in message attachments. Only supported attachment types are checked.
Parameters.Name: DocumentContainsWords

Parameters.Description: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.
Parameters.Name: SentToMemberOf

Parameters.Description: The ContentIsNotLabeled parameter specifies if the content is labeled. A True or False condition.
Parameters.Name: ContentIsNotLabeled

Parameters.Description: The SetHeader The SetHeader parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that adds or modifies a header field and value in the message header. You can specify multiple header name and value pairs separated by commas
Parameters.Name: SetHeader

Parameters.Description: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ContentExtensionMatchesWords

Parameters.Description: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.
Parameters.Name: ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords

Description: This resource configures a Sensitivity label policy in Purview.

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: Specify if this label policy should exist or not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.
Parameters.Name: AdvancedSettings

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include in the policy.
Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocation

Parameters.Description: The ExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to exclude when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: ExchangeLocationException

Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to include in the policy.
Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocation

Parameters.Description: The ModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to exclude when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: ModernGroupLocationException

Parameters.Description: The Labels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are associated with the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.
Parameters.Name: Labels

Parameters.Description: The AddExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to add in the existing policy.
Parameters.Name: AddExchangeLocation

Parameters.Description: The AddExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to add to exclusions when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: AddExchangeLocationException

Parameters.Description: The AddModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to include the policy.
Parameters.Name: AddModernGroupLocation

Parameters.Description: The AddModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to exclusions when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: AddModernGroupLocationException

Parameters.Description: The AddLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels to add to the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.
Parameters.Name: AddLabels

Parameters.Description: The RemoveExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove from the policy.
Parameters.Name: RemoveExchangeLocation

Parameters.Description: The RemoveExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: RemoveExchangeLocationException

Parameters.Description: The RemoveModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from the policy.
Parameters.Name: RemoveModernGroupLocation

Parameters.Description: The RemoveModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from excluded values when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.
Parameters.Name: RemoveModernGroupLocationException

Parameters.Description: The RemoveLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are removed from the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.
Parameters.Name: RemoveLabels

You can use this parameter only to exclude items from a hold policy, which excludes the specified item class from being held. Using this parameter won't exclude items from deletion policies. Parameters.Name: RetentionDuration

Parameters.Description: The RetentionDurationDisplayHint parameter specifies the units that are used to display the retention duration in the Security and Compliance Center. Valid values are: Days, Months or Years.
Parameters.Name: RetentionDurationDisplayHint

Description: This resource configures Sensitivity labels in Purview.

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: Specify if this rule should exist or not.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.
Parameters.Name: Comment

Parameters.Description: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.
Parameters.Name: AdvancedSettings

Parameters.Description: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name for the sensitivity label. The display name appears in the Microsoft Office and is used by Outlook users to select the appropriate sensitivity label before they send a message.
Parameters.Name: DisplayName

Parameters.Description: The LocaleSettings parameter specifies one or more localized label name or label Tooltips in different languages. Regions include all region codes supported in Office Client applications.
Parameters.Name: LocaleSettings

Parameters.Description: The ParentId parameter specifies the parent label that you want this label to be under (a sublabel). You can use any value that uniquely identifies the parent sensitivity label for example name.
Parameters.Name: ParentId

Parameters.Description: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the sensitivity label that determines the order of label processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority.
Parameters.Name: Priority

Parameters.Description: The ToolTip parameter specifies the default tooltip and sensitivity label description that's seen by users. It the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: Tooltip

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment parameter specifies the footer alignment.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the sensitivity label.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor parameter specifies the color of the footer text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the footer text.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the footer margin.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterText parameter specifies the footer text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingFooterText

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment parameter specifies the header alignment.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Content Marking Header action for the label.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor parameter specifies the color of the header text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the header text.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the header margin.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin

Parameters.Description: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText parameter specifies the header text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText

Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Watermarking Header action for the label.
Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor parameter specifies the color of the watermark text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx.
Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor

Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the watermark text.
Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize

Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingAlignment parameter specifies the watermark alignment.
Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingLayout

Parameters.Description: The ApplyWaterMarkingText parameter specifies the watermark text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
Parameters.Name: ApplyWaterMarkingText

Parameters.Description: The ContentType parameter specifies where the sensitivity label can be applied.
Parameters.Name: ContentType

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNever parameter specifies when the encrypted content expires. Valid values are integer or never.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNever

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionDoNotForward parameter specifies whether the Do Not Forward template is applied.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionDoNotForward

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionEncryptOnly parameter specifies whether the encrypt-only template is applied.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionEncryptOnly

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionEnabled parameter specifies whether encryption in enabled.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionEnabled

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionOfflineAccessDays parameter specifies the number of days that offline access is allowed.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionOfflineAccessDays

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionPromptUser parameter specifies whether to set the label with user defined permission in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionPromptUser

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionProtectionType parameter specifies the protection type for encryption.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionProtectionType

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionRightsDefinitions parameter specifies the rights users have when accessing protected. This parameter uses the syntax Identity1:Rights1,Rights2;Identity2:Rights3,Rights4. For example, john@contoso.com:VIEW,EDIT;microsoft.com:VIEW.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionRightsDefinitions

Parameters.Description: The EncryptionRightsUrl parameter specifies the URL for hold your own key (HYOK) protection.
Parameters.Name: EncryptionRightsUrl

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuestUsers parameter enables or disables access to guest users.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuestUsers

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGuestUsers parameter enables or disables email from guest users.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGuestUsers

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess parameter enables or disables full access.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess parameter enables or disables limited access.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionBlockAccess parameter blocks access.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionBlockAccess

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled parameter enables or disables the Site and Group Protection action for the labels.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy parameter specifies the privacy level for the label.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy

Parameters.Description: The SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType parameter specifies the external user sharing setting for the label.
Parameters.Name: SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType

Parameters.Description: The AutoLabelingSettings parameter specifies the conditions for label to be automatically applied to files and emails.
Parameters.Name: AutoLabelingSettings
