Parameters.Description: The Name of the Managed Property
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The Type of the Managed Property
Parameters.Name: Type

Parameters.Description: Description of the Managed Property
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: Enables querying against the content of the managed property.  The content of this managed property is included in the full-text index. For example, if the property is 'author', a simple query for 'Smith' returns items containing the word 'Smith' and items whose author property contains 'Smith'.
Parameters.Name: Searchable

Parameters.Description: Defines which full-text index the Managed Property is stored in.
Parameters.Name: FullTextIndex

Parameters.Description: Defines the context of a managed property within its full-text index.
Parameters.Name: FullTextContext

Parameters.Description: Enables querying against the specific Managed Property. The Managed Property field name must be included in the query, either specified in the query itself or included in the query programmatically. If the Managed Property is 'author', the query must contain 'author:Smith'.
Parameters.Name: Queryable

Parameters.Description: Enables the content of this managed property to be returned in search results. Enable this setting for managed properties that are relevant to present in search results.
Parameters.Name: Retrievable

Parameters.Description: Allow multiple values of the same type in this managed property. For example, if this is the 'author' managed property, and a document has multiple authors, each author name will be stored as a separate value in this managed property.
Parameters.Name: AllowMultipleValues

Parameters.Description: Yes: Enables using the property as a refiner for search results in the front end. You must manually configure the refiner in the web part. Yes - latent: Enables switching refinable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.
Parameters.Name: Refinable

Parameters.Description: Yes: Enables sorting the result set based on the property before the result set is returned. Use for example for large result sets that cannot be sorted and retrieved at the same time. Yes - latent: Enables switching sortable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.
Parameters.Name: Sortable

Parameters.Description: Enables this managed property to be returned for queries executed by anonymous users. Enable this setting for managed properties that do not contain sensitive information and are appropriate for anonymous users to view.
Parameters.Name: Safe

Parameters.Description: Define an alias for a managed property if you want to use the alias instead of the managed property name in queries and in search results. Use the original managed property and not the alias to map to a crawled property. Use an alias if you don't want to or don't have permission to create a new managed property.
Parameters.Name: Aliases

Parameters.Description: Enable to return results independent of letter casing and diacritics(for example accented characters) used in the query.
Parameters.Name: TokenNormalization

Parameters.Description: By default, search returns partial matches between queries against this managed property and its content. Select Complete Matching for search to return exact matches instead. If a managed property 'Title' contains 'Contoso Sites', only the query Title: 'Contoso Sites' will give a result.
Parameters.Name: CompleteMatching

Parameters.Description: By default, search depends on language when it breaks queries and content into parts (tokenization). Select language neutral tokenization if you have multilingual content and this managed property contains tags that are based on metadata term sets or other identifiers.
Parameters.Name: LanguageNeutralTokenization

Parameters.Description: By default, search tokenizes queries coarser than content. If a managed property 'ID' contains the string '1-23-456#7', and you query ID:'1-23', you might not get a partial match because search didn't break the query into small enough parts. Consider selecting finer query tokenization if the content of this managed property contains separators such as dots and dashes. Finer query tokenization makes queries against this managed property slower.
Parameters.Name: FinerQueryTokenization

Parameters.Description: Names of the crawled properties that are mapped to this managed property
Parameters.Name: MappedCrawledProperties

Parameters.Description: Enables the system to extract company name entities from the managed property when crawling new or updated items. Afterwards, the extracted entities can be used to set up refiners in the web part.
Parameters.Name: CompanyNameExtraction

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the Search Managed Property exists.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Color value in Hexadecimal.
Parameters.Name: Value

Description:
This resource allows users to configure and monitor the themes for
their SPO tenant.

To configure your SPO themes using this configuration resource requires you to
provide a color palette which at the end defines the colors of your theme.

You can use the SPO Theme builder under:

[https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder](https://aka.ms/spthemebuilder)

to create and preview your color palette.

Once you are satisfied with the color of your theme you can copy
the JSON Output and put it directly into your configuration.

More details on the theme schema can be found
[here](https://aka.ms/AboutSPOThemes)

Parameters.Description: The name of the theme, which appears in the theme picker UI and is also used by administrators and developers to refer to the theme in PowerShell cmdlets or calls to the SharePoint REST API.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: This value should be false for light themes and true for dark themes; it controls whether SharePoint uses dark or light theme colors to render text on colored backgrounds.
Parameters.Name: IsInverted

Parameters.Description: Specifies the color scheme which composes your theme.
Parameters.Name: Palette

Parameters.Description: Only accepted value is 'Present'.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Choose which apps and messaging extensions you want to be installed in your users' personal Teams environment and in meetings they create. Users can install other available apps from the Teams app store.

Parameters.Description: Choose which apps and meeting extensions you want to be installed in your users' personal Teams environment and in meetings they create. Users can install other available apps from the Teams app store.

Parameters.Description: Pinning an app displays the app in the app bar in Teams client. Admins can pin apps and they can allow users to pin apps. Pinning is used to highlight apps that are needed the most by users and promote ease of access.

If you turn this off, the user's existing app pins will be removed and replaced with the apps defined in this policy.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserPinning

Parameters.Description: This is also known as side loading. This setting determines if a user can upload a custom app package in the Teams app. Turning it on lets you create or develop a custom app to be used personally or across your organization without having to submit it to the Teams app store. Uploading a custom app also lets you test an app before you distribute it more widely by only assigning it to a single user or group of users.
Parameters.Name: AllowSideLoading

Description: Creates a new Teams call hold policy in your tenant. The Teams call hold policy is used to customize the call hold experience for Teams clients.

Parameters.Description: Identity of the Teams Calling Policy.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Description of the Teams Calling Policy.
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: Enables the user to use the AI Interpreter related features. Possible values are: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: AIInterpreter

Parameters.Description: The maximum amount a user can spend on outgoing PSTN calls, including all calls made through Pay-as-you-go Calling Plans and any overages on plans with bundled minutes.
Parameters.Name: CallingSpendUserLimit

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter lets you control how Copilot is used during calls and if transcription is needed to be turned on and saved after the call. Possible values: Enabled, EnabledWithTranscript, Disabled
Parameters.Name: Copilot

Parameters.Description: This setting allows an admin to enable or disable spend limits on PSTN calls for their user base.
Parameters.Name: EnableSpendLimits

Parameters.Description: Determines if MediaBypass is enabled for PSTN calls on specified Web platforms.
Parameters.Name: EnableWebPstnMediaBypass

Parameters.Description: This setting controls whether users must provide or obtain explicit consent before recording a 1:1 PSTN or Teams call. When enabled, both parties will receive a notification, and consent must be given before recording starts. Possible values: Enabled: Requires users to give and obtain explicit consent before starting a call recording. Disabled: Users are not required to obtain explicit consent before recording starts.
Parameters.Name: ExplicitRecordingConsent

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter lets you control how inbound federated calls should be routed. Possible values are: RegularIncoming, Unanswered, Voicemail.
Parameters.Name: InboundFederatedCallRoutingTreatment

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter lets you control how inbound PSTN calls should be routed. Possible values are: RegularIncoming, Unanswered, Voicemail, UserOverride.
Parameters.Name: InboundPstnCallRoutingTreatment

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter allows you to set the PopoutForIncomingPstnCalls setting's URL path of the website to launch upon receiving incoming PSTN calls. This parameter accepts an HTTPS URL with less than 1024 characters. The URL can contain a {phone} placeholder that is replaced with the caller's PSTN number in E.164 format when launched.
Parameters.Name: PopoutAppPathForIncomingPstnCalls

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter allows you to control the tenant users' ability to launch an external website URL automatically in the browser window upon incoming PSTN calls for specific users or user groups. Possible values are: Enabled, Disabled.
Parameters.Name: PopoutForIncomingPstnCalls

Parameters.Description: Allows users to use real time text during a call, allowing them to communicate by typing their messages in real time. Possible values are: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: RealTimeText

Parameters.Description: Controls if Teams calls are shown in the call log.
Parameters.Name: ShowTeamsCallsInCallLog

Parameters.Description: Enables the user to use the voice simulation feature while being AI interpreted. Possible values are: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: VoiceSimulationInInterpreter

Parameters.Description: Controls all calling capabilities in Teams. Turning this off will turn off all calling functionality in Teams. If you use Skype for Business for calling, this policy will not affect calling functionality in Skype for Business.
Parameters.Name: AllowPrivateCalling

Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to voice mail. Valid options are: AlwaysEnabled, AlwaysDisabled, UserOverride.
Parameters.Name: AllowVoicemail

Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to call groups.
Parameters.Name: AllowCallGroups

Parameters.Description: Enables inbound calls to be routed to delegates; allows delegates to make outbound calls on behalf of the users for whom they have delegated permissions.
Parameters.Name: AllowDelegation

Parameters.Description: Enables call forwarding or simultaneous ringing of inbound calls to other users in your tenant.
Parameters.Name: AllowCallForwardingToUser

Parameters.Description: Enables call forwarding or simultaneous ringing of inbound calls to any phone number.
Parameters.Name: AllowCallForwardingToPhone

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter provides the ability to configure call redirection capabilities on Teams phones.
Parameters.Name: AllowCallRedirect

Parameters.Description: Determines whether the user is allowed to use SIP devices for calling on behalf of a Teams client.
Parameters.Name: AllowSIPDevicesCalling

Parameters.Description: Allows PSTN calling from the Team web client
Parameters.Name: AllowWebPSTNCalling

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter to True will send calls through PSTN and incur charges rather than going through the network and bypassing the tolls.
Parameters.Name: PreventTollBypass

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter lets you configure how incoming calls are handled when a user is already in a call or conference or has a call placed on hold. New or incoming calls will be rejected with a busy signal. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled and Unanswered.
Parameters.Name: BusyOnBusyEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Sets the expiration of the recorded 1:1 calls.
Parameters.Name: CallRecordingExpirationDays

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter allows you to turn on or turn off music on hold when a PSTN caller is placed on hold. It is turned on by default. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride. For now setting the value to UserOverride is the same as Enabled. This setting does not apply to call park and SLA boss delegate features. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride.
Parameters.Name: MusicOnHoldEnabledType

Parameters.Description: This parameter is not available for use. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled, UserOverride.
Parameters.Name: SafeTransferEnabled

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter to True will allows 1:1 Calls to be recorded.
Parameters.Name: AllowCloudRecordingForCalls

Parameters.Description: Determines whether post-meeting captions and transcriptions are allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowTranscriptionforCalling

Parameters.Description: Determines whether real-time captions are available for the user in Teams meetings. Set this to DisabledUserOverride to allow user to turn on live captions. Set this to Disabled to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: LiveCaptionsEnabledTypeForCalling

Parameters.Description: This setting allows the tenant admin to enable or disable the Auto-Answer setting. Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled.
Parameters.Name: AutoAnswerEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Setting this parameter determines whether calls identified as Spam will be rejected or not (probably). Valid options are: Enabled, Disabled.
Parameters.Name: SpamFilteringEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policyexists, absent ensures it is removed.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Description:

More information: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-calling-policy/>

Parameters.Description: A unique identifier for the policy - this will be used to retrieve the policy later on to assign it to specific users.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: If set to true, customers will be able to leverage the call park feature to place calls on hold and then decide how the call should be handled - transferred to another department, retrieved using the same phone, or retrieved using a different phone.
Parameters.Name: AllowCallPark

Parameters.Description: Description of the Teams Call Park Policy.
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: Specify the number of seconds to wait before ringing the parker when the parked call hasn't been picked up. Value can be from 120 to 1800 (seconds).
Parameters.Name: ParkTimeoutSeconds

Parameters.Description: Specify the maximum value that a rendered pickup code can take. Value can be from 10 to 9999. Note: PickupRangeStart must be smaller than PickupRangeEnd.
Parameters.Name: PickupRangeEnd

Parameters.Description: Specify the minimum value that a rendered pickup code can take. Value can be from 10 to 9999.
Parameters.Name: PickupRangeStart

Description: This resource configures a Teams Call Park Policy. Call park allows enterprise voice customers to place a call on hold and then perform a number of actions on that call: transfer to another department, retrieve via the same phone, or retrieve via a different Teams phone.

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the Call Queue.
Parameters.Name: Name

Parameters.Description: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the Call Queue.
Parameters.Name: AgentAlertTime

Parameters.Description: The AllowOptOut parameter indicates whether or not agents can opt in or opt out from taking calls from a Call Queue.
Parameters.Name: AllowOptOut

Parameters.Description: The DistributionLists parameter lets you add all the members of the distribution lists to the Call Queue. This is a list of distribution list GUIDs. A service wide configurable maximum number of DLs per Call Queue are allowed. Only the first N (service wide configurable) agents from all distribution lists combined are considered for accepting the call. Nested DLs are supported. O365 Groups can also be used to add members to the Call Queue.
Parameters.Name: DistributionLists

Parameters.Description: The UseDefaultMusicOnHold parameter indicates that this Call Queue uses the default music on hold. This parameter cannot be specified together with MusicOnHoldAudioFileId.
Parameters.Name: UseDefaultMusicOnHold

Parameters.Description: The WelcomeMusicAudioFileId parameter represents the audio file to play when callers are connected with the Call Queue. This is the unique identifier of the audio file.
Parameters.Name: WelcomeMusicAudioFileId

Parameters.Description: The MusicOnHoldFileContent parameter represents music to play when callers are placed on hold. This is the unique identifier of the audio file. This parameter is required if the UseDefaultMusicOnHold parameter is not specified.
Parameters.Name: MusicOnHoldAudioFileId

Parameters.Description: The OverflowAction parameter designates the action to take if the overflow threshold is reached. The OverflowAction property must be set to one of the following values: DisconnectWithBusy, Forward, Voicemail, and SharedVoicemail. The default value is DisconnectWithBusy.
Parameters.Name: OverflowAction

Parameters.Description: The OverflowActionTarget parameter represents the target of the overflow action. If the OverFlowAction is set to Forward, this parameter must be set to a Guid or a telephone number with a mandatory 'tel:' prefix. If the OverflowAction is set to SharedVoicemail, this parameter must be set to a group ID (Microsoft 365, Distribution list, or Mail-enabled security). Otherwise, this parameter is optional.
Parameters.Name: OverflowActionTarget

Parameters.Description: The OverflowThreshold parameter defines the number of calls that can be in the queue at any one time before the overflow action is triggered. The OverflowThreshold can be any integer value between 0 and 200, inclusive. A value of 0 causes calls not to reach agents and the overflow action to be taken immediately.
Parameters.Name: OverflowThreshold

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutAction parameter defines the action to take if the timeout threshold is reached. The TimeoutAction property must be set to one of the following values: Disconnect, Forward, Voicemail, and SharedVoicemail. The default value is Disconnect.
Parameters.Name: TimeoutAction

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutActionTarget represents the target of the timeout action. If the TimeoutAction is set to Forward, this parameter must be set to a Guid or a telephone number with a mandatory 'tel:' prefix. If the TimeoutAction is set to SharedVoicemail, this parameter must be set to an Office 365 Group ID. Otherwise, this field is optional.
Parameters.Name: TimeoutActionTarget

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutThreshold parameter defines the time (in seconds) that a call can be in the queue before that call times out. At that point, the system will take the action specified by the TimeoutAction parameter. The TimeoutThreshold can be any integer value between 0 and 2700 seconds (inclusive), and is rounded to the nearest 15th interval. For example, if set to 47 seconds, then it is rounded down to 45. If set to 0, welcome music is played, and then the timeout action will be taken.
Parameters.Name: TimeoutThreshold

Parameters.Description: The RoutingMethod defines how agents will be called in a Call Queue. If the routing method is set to Serial, then agents will be called one at a time. If the routing method is set to Attendant, then agents will be called in parallel. If routing method is set to RoundRobin, the agents will be called using Round Robin strategy so that all agents share the call-load equally. If routing method is set to LongestIdle, the agents will be called based on their idle time, i.e., the agent that has been idle for the longest period will be called.
Parameters.Name: RoutingMethod

Parameters.Description: The PresenceBasedRouting parameter indicates whether or not presence based routing will be applied while call being routed to Call Queue agents. When set to False, calls will be routed to agents who have opted in to receive calls, regardless of their presence state. When set to True, opted-in agents will receive calls only when their presence state is Available.
Parameters.Name: PresenceBasedRouting

Parameters.Description: The ConferenceMode parameter indicates whether or not Conference mode will be applied on calls for this Call queue. Conference mode significantly reduces the amount of time it takes for a caller to be connected to an agent, after the agent accepts the call.
Parameters.Name: ConferenceMode

Parameters.Description: The Users parameter lets you add agents to the Call Queue. This parameter expects a list of user unique identifiers (GUID).
Parameters.Name: Users

Parameters.Description: The LanguageId parameter indicates the language that is used to play shared voicemail prompts. This parameter becomes a required parameter If either OverflowAction or TimeoutAction is set to SharedVoicemail. You can query the supported languages using the Get-CsAutoAttendantSupportedLanguage cmdlet.
Parameters.Name: LanguageId

Parameters.Description: The OboResourceAccountIds parameter lets you add resource account with phone number to the Call Queue. The agents in the Call Queue will be able to make outbound calls using the phone number on the resource accounts. This is a list of resource account GUIDs. Only Call Queue managed by a Teams Channel will be able to use this feature.
Parameters.Name: OboResourceAccountIds

Parameters.Description: The OverflowDisconnectTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being disconnected due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowDisconnectTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowDisconnectAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being disconnected due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowDisconnectAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectPersonTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person in the organization due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectPersonTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectPersonAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person in the organization due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectPersonAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectVoiceAppsTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a voice application due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectVoiceAppTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectVoiceAppAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a voice application due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectVoiceAppAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectPhoneNumberTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to an external PSTN phone number due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectPhoneNumberTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectPhoneNumberAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to an external PSTN phone number due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectPhoneNumberAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person's voicemail due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectVoiceMailAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person's voicemail due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowRedirectVoicemailAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowRedirectVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person's voicemail due to overflow.
Parameters.Name: OverflowSharedVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt

Parameters.Description: The OverflowSharedVoicemailAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is to be played as a greeting to the caller when transferred to shared voicemail on overflow. This parameter becomes a required parameter when OverflowAction is SharedVoicemail and OverflowSharedVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt is null.
Parameters.Name: OverflowSharedVoicemailAudioFilePrompt

Parameters.Description: The EnableOverflowSharedVoicemailTranscription parameter is used to turn on transcription for voicemails left by a caller on overflow. This parameter is only applicable when OverflowAction is set to SharedVoicemail.
Parameters.Name: EnableOverflowSharedVoicemailTranscription

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutRedirectVoiceAppAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a voice application due to timeout.

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutRedirectVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person's voicemail due to timeout.

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutRedirectVoiceMailAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is played to the caller when being redirected to a person's voicemail due to timeout.

Parameters.Description: The TimeoutSharedVoicemailTextToSpeechPrompt parameter indicates the Text-to-Speech (TTS) prompt which is to be played as a greeting to the caller when transferred to shared voicemail on timeout. Parameters.Description: The TimeoutSharedVoicemailAudioFilePrompt parameter indicates the unique identifier for the Audio file prompt which is to be played as a greeting to the caller when transferred to shared voicemail on timeout. Parameters.Description: The EnableTimeoutSharedVoicemailTranscription parameter is used to turn on transcription for voicemails left by a caller on timeout. This parameter is only applicable when TimeoutAction is set to SharedVoicemail.

Parameters.Name: AllowGoogleDrive

Parameters.Description: Designates whether or not guest users in your organization will have access to the Teams client. If $true, guests in your tenant will be able to access the Teams client. Note that this setting has a core dependency on Guest Access being enabled in your Office 365 tenant.
Parameters.Name: AllowGuestUser

Parameters.Description: When set to $true, users will be able to see the organizational chart icon other users' contact cards, and when clicked, this icon will display the detailed organizational chart.
Parameters.Name: AllowOrganizationTab

Parameters.Description: Surface Hub uses a device account to provide email and collaboration services (IM, video, voice). This device account is used as the originating identity (the from party) when sending email, IM, and placing calls. As this account is not coming from an individual, identifiable user, it is deemed anonymous because it originated from the Surface Hub's device account. If set to $true, these device accounts will be able to send chat messages in Skype for Business Online (does not apply to Microsoft Teams).
Parameters.Name: AllowResourceAccountSendMessage

Parameters.Description: When set to True, Supervised Chat is enabled for the tenant.
Parameters.Name: AllowRoleBasedChatPermissions

Parameters.Description: If set to $true, the Exchange address book policy (ABP) will be used to provide customized view of the global address book for each user. This is only a virtual separation and not a legal separation.

Parameters.Description: Designates whether users are able to leverage ShareFile as a third party storage solution in Microsoft Teams. If $true, users will be able to add ShareFile in the client and interact with the files stored there.
Parameters.Name: AllowShareFile

Parameters.Description: When set to $true, Teams conversations automatically show up in Skype for Business for users that aren't enabled for Teams.
Parameters.Name: AllowSkypeBusinessInterop

Parameters.Description: Designates whether users are able to leverage Egnyte as a third party storage solution in Microsoft Teams. If $true, users will be able to add Egnyte in the client and interact with the files stored there.
Parameters.Name: AllowEgnyte

Parameters.Description: This setting applies only to Skype for Business Online (not Microsoft Teams) and defines whether the user must provide a secondary form of authentication to access the meeting content from a resource device account. Parameters.Description: If set to $true, Teams meeting broadcast streams are enabled to take advantage of the network and bandwidth management capabilities of your Software Defined Network (SDN) provider.
Parameters.Name: AllowSdnProviderForBroadcastMeeting

Parameters.Description: Specifies the Software Defined Network (SDN) provider's name. This parameter is only required if AllowSdnProviderForBroadcastMeeting is set to $true.
Parameters.Name: SdnProviderName

Parameters.Name: SdnLicenseId

Parameters.Description: Specifies the Software Defined Network (SDN) provider's authentication token which is required to use their SDN license. This is required by some SDN providers who will give you the required token. This parameter is only required if AllowSdnProviderForBroadcastMeeting is set to $true.
Parameters.Name: SdnApiToken

Description:
This resource is used to configure the Teams Meeting Broadcast Settings.

More information: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-live-events/configure-teams-live-events/>

Parameters.Description: The identifier of the Teams Meeting Broadcast Policy.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether this user can create broadcast events in Teams. This setting impacts broadcasts that use both self-service and external encoder production methods.
Parameters.Name: AllowBroadcastScheduling

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether real-time transcription and translation can be enabled in the broadcast event. Note: this setting is applicable to broadcast events that use Teams Meeting production only and does not apply when external encoder is used as production method.
Parameters.Name: AllowBroadcastTranscription

Parameters.Description: Specifies the attendee visibility mode of the broadcast events created by this user.  This setting controls who can watch the broadcast event - e.g. anyone can watch this event including anonymous users or only authenticated users in my company can watch the event.  Note: this setting is applicable to broadcast events that use Teams Meeting production only and does not apply when external encoder is used as production method.
Parameters.Name: BroadcastAttendeeVisibilityMode

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether broadcast events created by this user are always recorded, never recorded or user can choose whether to record or not. Note: this setting is applicable to broadcast events that use Teams Meeting production only and does not apply when external encoder is used as production method.
Parameters.Name: BroadcastRecordingMode

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the Policy exists, absent ensures it is removed
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Description:
This resource is used to configure the Teams Meeting Broadcast Policies.

More information: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-live-events/set-up-for-teams-live-events/>

Parameters.Description: The only valid input is Global - the tenant wide configuration
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: URL to a logo image. This would be included in the meeting invite. Please ensure this URL is publicly accessible for invites that go beyond your federation boundaries.
Parameters.Name: LogoURL

Parameters.Description: URL to a website containing legal information and meeting disclaimers. This would be included in the meeting invite. Please ensure this URL is publicly accessible for invites that go beyond your federation boundaries.
Parameters.Name: LegalURL

Parameters.Description: URL to a website where users can obtain assistance on joining the meeting.This would be included in the meeting invite. Please ensure this URL is publicly accessible for invites that go beyond your federation boundaries.
Parameters.Name: HelpURL

Parameters.Description: Text to be used on custom meeting invitations.
Parameters.Name: CustomFooterText

Parameters.Description: Determines whether anonymous users are blocked from joining meetings in the tenant. Set this to TRUE to block anonymous users from joining. Set this to FALSE to allow anonymous users to join meetings.
Parameters.Name: DisableAnonymousJoin

Parameters.Description: Determines if anonymous users can interact with apps in meetings. Set to TRUE to disable App interaction.
Parameters.Name: DisableAppInteractionForAnonymousUsers

Parameters.Description: Determines whether Quality of Service Marking for real-time media (audio, video, screen/app sharing) is enabled in the tenant. Set this to TRUE to enable and FALSE to disable.
Parameters.Name: EnableQoS

Parameters.Description: Determines if anonymous participants receive surveys to provide feedback about their meeting experience. Set to Disabled to disable anonymous meeting participants to receive surveys. Set to Enabled to allow anonymous meeting participants to receive surveys. Possible values: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: FeedbackSurveyForAnonymousUsers

Parameters.Description: When set to True, users within the Tenant will have their presenter role capabilities limited. When set to False, the presenter role capabilities will not be impacted and will remain as is.
Parameters.Name: LimitPresenterRolePermissions

Parameters.Description: Determines the starting port number for client audio. Minimum allowed value: 1024 Maximum allowed value: 65535 Default value: 50000.
Parameters.Name: ClientAudioPort

Parameters.Description: Determines the total number of ports available for client audio. Default value is 20.
Parameters.Name: ClientAudioPortRange

Parameters.Description: Determines the starting port number for client video. Minimum allowed value: 1024 Maximum allowed value: 65535 Default value: 50020.
Parameters.Name: ClientVideoPort

Parameters.Description: Determines the total number of ports available for client video. Default value is 20.
Parameters.Name: ClientVideoPortRange

Parameters.Description: Determines the starting port number for client screen sharing or application sharing. Minimum allowed value: 1024 Maximum allowed value: 65535 Default value: 50040.
Parameters.Name: ClientAppSharingPort

Parameters.Description: Determines whether custom media port and range selections need to be enforced. When set to True, clients will use the specified port range for media traffic. When set to False (the default value) for any available port (from port 1024 through port 65535) will be used to accommodate media traffic.
Parameters.Name: ClientMediaPortRangeEnabled

Parameters.Description: Determines the total number of ports available for client sharing or application sharing. Default value is 20.
Parameters.Name: ClientAppSharingPortRange

Parameters.Name: Credential

Description:
This resource is used to configure the Teams Meeting Configuration.

More information: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-settings-in-teams/>

Parameters.Description: Identity of the Teams Meeting Policy.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Description of the Teams Meeting Policy.
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: Enables the user to use the AI Interpreter related features. Possible values: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: AIInterpreter

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can use the Annotation feature
Parameters.Name: AllowAnnotations

Parameters.Description: CURRENTLY DISABLED: Determines whether anonymous users can use the Call Me At feature for meeting audio.
Parameters.Name: AllowAnonymousUsersToDialOut

Parameters.Description: Determines whether anonymous users can join the meetings that impacted users organize. Set this to TRUE to allow anonymous users to join a meeting. Set this to FALSE to prohibit them from joining a meeting.
Parameters.Name: AllowAnonymousUsersToJoinMeeting

Parameters.Description: Determines whether anonymous users can initiate a meeting. Set this to TRUE to allow anonymous users to initiate a meeting. Set this to FALSE to prohibit them from initiating a meeting.
Parameters.Name: AllowAnonymousUsersToStartMeeting

Parameters.Description: If admins disable avatars in 2D meetings, then users cannot represent themselves as avatars in the Gallery view. This does not disable avatars in Immersive view.
Parameters.Name: AllowAvatarsInGallery

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not meetings created by users with this policy are able to utilize the Breakout Rooms feature.
Parameters.Name: AllowBreakoutRooms

Parameters.Description: This setting will enable Tenant Admins to enable/disable the sharing of location data necessary to provide the end of meeting carbon summary screen for either the entire tenant or for a particular user. If set to True the meeting organizer will share their location to the client of the participant to enable the calculation of distance and the resulting carbon.
Parameters.Name: AllowCarbonSummary

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can add a URL for captions from a Communications Access Real-Time Translation (CART) captioner for providing real-time captions in meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowCartCaptionsScheduling

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can schedule channel meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to schedule channel meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from scheduling channel meetings. Note this only restricts from scheduling and not from joining a meeting scheduled by another user.
Parameters.Name: AllowChannelMeetingScheduling

Parameters.Description: Determines whether cloud recording is allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to be able to record meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from recording meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowCloudRecording

Parameters.Description: This setting will allow admins to choose which users will be able to use the Document Collaboration feature.
Parameters.Name: AllowDocumentCollaboration

Parameters.Description: Enables the use of RTMP-In in Teams meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowedStreamingMediaInput

Parameters.Description: Controls which users should have ability to see the meeting info details on join screen. 'None' option should disable the feature completely. Possible Values: UsersAllowedToByPassTheLobby: Users who are able to bypass lobby can see the meeting info details. Everyone: All meeting participants can see the meeting info details.
Parameters.Name: AllowedUsersForMeetingDetails

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not a meeting Organizer can track join and leave times for all users within their meetings as well as download a roster.
Parameters.Name: AllowEngagementReport

Parameters.Description: Determines whether external participants can request or give control of screen sharing during meetings scheduled by this user. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to be able to give or request control. Set this to FALSE to prohibit an external user from giving or requesting control in a meeting.
Parameters.Name: AllowExternalParticipantGiveRequestControl

Parameters.Description: If admins have disabled avatars, this does not disable using avatars in Immersive view on Teams desktop or web. Additionally, it does not prevent users from joining the Teams meeting on VR headsets.
Parameters.Name: AllowImmersiveView

Parameters.Description: Determines whether audio is enabled in a user's meetings or calls. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to share their audioo. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from sharing their audio.
Parameters.Name: AllowIPAudio

Parameters.Description: Determines whether video is enabled in a user's meetings or calls. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to share their video. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from sharing their video.
Parameters.Name: AllowIPVideo

Parameters.Description: This setting will allow admins to allow users the option of turning on Meeting Coach during meetings, which provides users with private personalized feedback on their communication and inclusivity.
Parameters.Name: AllowMeetingCoach

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not meetings created by users with this policy are able to utilize the Meeting Reactions feature.
Parameters.Name: AllowMeetingReactions

Parameters.Description: Controls if a user can create a webinar meeting. The default value is True.
Parameters.Name: AllowMeetingRegistration

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can start ad-hoc meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to start ad-hoc meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from starting ad-hoc meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowMeetNow

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is able to use NDI (Network Device Interface) in meetings - both for output and input streams.
Parameters.Name: AllowNDIStreaming

Parameters.Description: Determines whether network configuration setting lookups can be made by users who are not Enterprise Voice enabled. It is used to enable Network Roaming policies.
Parameters.Name: AllowNetworkConfigurationSettingsLookup

Parameters.Description: Determines whether organizers can override lobby settings for both VOIP and PSTN. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowOrganizersToOverrideLobbySettings

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can schedule Teams Meetings in Outlook desktop client. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to be able to schedule Teams meetings in Outlook client. Set this to FALSE to prohibit a user from scheduling Teams meeting in Outlook client.
Parameters.Name: AllowOutlookAddIn

Parameters.Description: Determines whether participants can request or give control of screen sharing during meetings scheduled by this user. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to be able to give or request control. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from giving, requesting control in a meeting.
Parameters.Name: AllowParticipantGiveRequestControl

Parameters.Description: Determines whether Powerpoint sharing is allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowPowerPointSharing

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can schedule private meetings. Note this only restricts from scheduling and not from joining a meeting scheduled by another user. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to schedule private meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from scheduling private meetings. Note this only restricts from scheduling and not from joining a meeting scheduled by another user.
Parameters.Name: AllowPrivateMeetingScheduling

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user can start private ad-hoc meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to start private ad-hoc meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from starting private ad-hoc meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowPrivateMeetNow

Parameters.Description: Determines whether PSTN users should be automatically admitted to the meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow the PSTN user to be able bypass the meetinglobby. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the PSTN user from bypassing the meetinglobby.
Parameters.Name: AllowPSTNUsersToBypassLobby

Parameters.Description: Determines whether cloud recording can be stored out of region for go-local tenants where recording is not yet enabled.
Parameters.Name: AllowRecordingStorageOutsideRegion

Parameters.Description: Determines whether users are allowed to take shared notes. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowSharedNotes

Parameters.Description: This policy setting allows for the extraction of AI-Assisted Action Items/Tasks from the Meeting Transcript.
Parameters.Name: AllowTasksFromTranscript

Parameters.Description: Determines whether real-time and/or post-meeting captions and transcriptions are allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowTranscription

Parameters.Description: Determines what types of external meetings users can join. Enabled is able join all external meetings.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserToJoinExternalMeeting

Parameters.Description: This setting allows scheduling meetings with customized watermarking for video enabled.
Parameters.Name: AllowWatermarkCustomizationForCameraVideo

Parameters.Description: This setting allows scheduling meetings with customized watermarking for screen sharing enabled.
Parameters.Name: AllowWatermarkCustomizationForScreenSharing

Parameters.Description: This setting allows scheduling meetings with watermarking for video enabled.
Parameters.Name: AllowWatermarkForCameraVideo

Parameters.Description: This setting allows scheduling meetings with watermarking for screen sharing enabled.
Parameters.Name: AllowWatermarkForScreenSharing

Parameters.Description: Determines whether whiteboard is allowed in a user's meetings. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowWhiteboard

Parameters.Description: Determines how anonymous users will be authenticated when joining a meeting. Possible values are: OneTimePasscode, if you would like anonymous users to be sent a one time passcode to their email when joining a meeting. None, if you would like to disable authentication for anonymous users joining a meeting
Parameters.Name: AnonymousUserAuthenticationMethod

Parameters.Description: This setting will allow admins to enable or disable Masked Attendee mode in Meetings. Masked Attendee meetings will hide attendees' identifying information (e.g., name, contact information, profile photo).
Parameters.Name: AttendeeIdentityMasking

Parameters.Description: The setting controls whether recording notification is played to all attendees or just PSTN users.
Parameters.Name: AudibleRecordingNotification

Parameters.Description: Determines what types of participants will automatically be added to meetings organized by this user. Set this to EveryoneInCompany if you would like meetings to place every external user in the lobby but allow all users in the company to join the meeting immediately. Set this to Everyone if you'd like to admit anonymous users by default. Set this to EveryoneInSameAndFederatedCompany if you would like meetings to allow federated users to join like your company's users, but place all other external users in a lobby. Set this to InvitedUsers if you would like meetings to allow only the invited users.
Parameters.Name: AutoAdmittedUsers

Parameters.Description: This setting gives admins the ability to auto-start Copilot.
Parameters.Name: AutomaticallyStartCopilot

Parameters.Description: This setting will enable Tenant Admins to turn on/off auto recording feature.
Parameters.Name: AutoRecording

Parameters.Description: A user can join a Teams meeting anonymously using a Teams client or using a custom application built using Azure Communication Services. When anonymous meeting join is enabled, both types of clients may be used by default. This optional parameter can be used to block one of the client types that can be used. The allowed values are ACS (to block the use of Azure Communication Services clients) or Teams (to block the use of Teams clients). Both can also be specified, separated by a comma, but this is equivalent to disabling anonymous join completely.
Parameters.Name: BlockedAnonymousJoinClientTypes

Parameters.Description: Require a verification check for meeting join.
Parameters.Name: CaptchaVerificationForMeetingJoin

Parameters.Description: Determines how channel meeting recordings are saved, permissioned, and who can download them.
Parameters.Name: ChannelRecordingDownload

Parameters.Description: Allows external connections of thirdparty apps to Microsoft Teams.
Parameters.Name: ConnectToMeetingControls

Parameters.Description: This policy allows admins to determine whether the user can share content in meetings organized by external organizations. The user should have a Teams Premium license to be protected under this policy.
Parameters.Name: ContentSharingInExternalMeetings

Parameters.Description: This setting allows the admin to choose whether Copilot will be enabled with a persisted transcript or a non-persisted transcript.
Parameters.Name: Copilot

Parameters.Description: This parameter enables a setting that controls a meeting option which allows users to disable right-click or Ctrl+C to copy, Copy link, Forward message, and Share to Outlook for meeting chat messages.
Parameters.Name: CopyRestriction

Parameters.Description: Determines if users can change the default value of the Who can present? setting in Meeting options in the Teams client. This policy setting affects all meetings, including Meet Now meetings.
Parameters.Name: DesignatedPresenterRoleMode

Parameters.Description: Allows the admin to enable sensitive content detection during screen share.
Parameters.Name: DetectSensitiveContentDuringScreenSharing

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not users will be able to enroll/capture their Biometric data: Face & Voice.
Parameters.Name: EnrollUserOverride

Parameters.Description: This setting will enable Tenant Admins to turn on/off Explicit Recording Consent feature.
Parameters.Name: ExplicitRecordingConsent

Parameters.Description: Determines whether the user is allowed to join external meetings.
Parameters.Name: ExternalMeetingJoin

Parameters.Description: This policy controls what kind of information get shown for the user's attendance in attendance report/dashboard.
Parameters.Name: InfoShownInReportMode

Parameters.Description: Determines whether audio can be turned on in meetings and group calls.
Parameters.Name: IPAudioMode

Parameters.Description: Determines whether video can be turned on in meetings and group calls. Can only be enabled if IPAudioMode is enabled
Parameters.Name: IPVideoMode

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user should have the option to view live captions or not in a meeting.
Parameters.Name: LiveCaptionsEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Determines how meeting organizers can configure a meeting for language interpretation, select attendees of the meeting to become interpreters that other attendees can select and listen to the real-time translation they provide.
Parameters.Name: LiveInterpretationEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Determines whether you provide support for your users to stream their Teams meetings to large audiences through Real-Time Messaging Protocol (RTMP).
Parameters.Name: LiveStreamingMode

Parameters.Description: Determines whether chat messages are allowed in the lobby. Possible values are: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: LobbyChat

Parameters.Description: Determines the media bit rate for audio/video/app sharing transmissions in meetings.
Parameters.Name: MediaBitRateKb

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not Chat will be enabled, enabled except anonymous or disabled for meetings.
Parameters.Name: MeetingChatEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Controls how the join information in meeting invitations is displayed by enforcing a common language or enabling up to two languages to be displayed. Note: All Teams supported languages can be specified using language codes.
Parameters.Name: MeetingInviteLanguages

Parameters.Description: Specifies the number of days before meeting recordings will expire and move to the recycle bin. Value can be from 1 to 99,999 days. NOTE: You may opt to set Meeting Recordings to never expire by entering the value -1.
Parameters.Name: NewMeetingRecordingExpirationDays

Parameters.Description: Control Noises Supression Feature for PST legs joining a meeting. Possible Values: MicrosoftDefault, Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: NoiseSuppressionForDialInParticipants

Parameters.Description: This setting will enable Tenant Admins to turn on/off participant renaming feature.
Parameters.Name: ParticipantNameChange

Parameters.Description: Determines whether participants can give control of presentation slides during meetings scheduled by this user. Set the type of users you want to be able to give control and be given control of presentation slides in meetings. Users excluded from the selected group will be prohibited from giving control, or being given control, in a meeting. Possible Values: Everyone: Anyone in the meeting can give or take control. EveryoneInOrganization: Only internal AAD users and Multi-Tenant Organization (MTO) users can give or take control. EveryoneInOrganizationAndGuests: Only those who are Guests to the tenant, MTO users, and internal AAD users can give or take control. None: No one in the meeting can give or take control
Parameters.Name: ParticipantSlideControl

Parameters.Description: Determines which Outlook Add-in the user will get as preferred Meeting provider(TeamsAndSfb or Teams).
Parameters.Name: PreferredMeetingProviderForIslandsMode

Parameters.Description: This setting enables Microsoft 365 Tenant Admins to Enable or Disable the Questions and Answers experience (Q+A).
Parameters.Name: QnAEngagementMode

Parameters.Description: Allows users to use real time text during a meeting, allowing them to communicate by typing their messages in real time. Possible Values: Enabled: User is allowed to turn on real time text. Disabled: User is not allowed to turn on real time text.
Parameters.Name: RealTimeText

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not biometric data will be used to distinguish and or attribute in the transcript.
Parameters.Name: RoomAttributeUserOverride

Parameters.Description: Determines if people recognition option is enabled for Teams Rooms. Enabling requires the RoomAttributeUserOverride to be Attribute for allowing individual voice and face profiles to be used for recognition in meetings.
Parameters.Name: RoomPeopleNameUserOverride

Parameters.Description: Determines the mode in which a user can share a screen in calls or meetings. Set this to SingleApplication to allow the user to share an application at a given point in time. Set this to EntireScreen to allow the user to share anything on their screens. Set this to Disabled to prohibit the user from sharing their screens.
Parameters.Name: ScreenSharingMode

Parameters.Description: Participants can sign up for text message meeting reminders.
Parameters.Name: SmsNotifications

Parameters.Description: Determines if users are identified in transcriptions and if they can change the value of the Automatically identify me in meeting captions and transcripts setting.
Parameters.Name: SpeakerAttributionMode

Parameters.Description: Controls if Teams uses overflow capability once a meeting reaches its capacity (1,000 users with full functionality).
Parameters.Name: StreamingAttendeeMode

Parameters.Description: Determines whether or not meetings created by users with this policy are able to utilize the Camera Far-End PTZ Mode.
Parameters.Name: TeamsCameraFarEndPTZMode

Parameters.Description: This policy controls who can admit from the lobby.
Parameters.Name: UsersCanAdmitFromLobby

Parameters.Description: Determines whether you provide support for your users to enable voice isolation in Teams meeting calls.
Parameters.Name: VoiceIsolation

Parameters.Description: Enables the user to use the voice simulation feature while being AI interpreted. Possible Values: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: VoiceSimulationInInterpreter

Parameters.Description: Determines the meeting experience and watermark content of an anonymous user.
Parameters.Name: WatermarkForAnonymousUsers

Parameters.Description: Allows the transparency of watermark to be customizable. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Parameters.Name: WatermarkForCameraVideoOpacity

Parameters.Description: Allows the pattern design of watermark to be customizable.
Parameters.Name: WatermarkForCameraVideoPattern

Parameters.Description: Allows the transparency of watermark to be customizable. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Parameters.Name: WatermarkForScreenSharingOpacity

Parameters.Description: Allows the pattern design of watermark to be customizable.
Parameters.Name: WatermarkForScreenSharingPattern

Parameters.Description: Determines the background effects that a user can configure in the Teams client.
Parameters.Name: VideoFiltersMode

Parameters.Description: Specifies who can attend and register for webinars.
Parameters.Name: WhoCanRegister

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Teams Global Admin.
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.
Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Description: Name of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication. Format contoso.onmicrosoft.com
Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.
Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: AccessTokens

Description:
This resource configures the Teams Meeting Policies.

More information: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-policies-overview/>

Parameters.Description: Only valid value is 'Yes'.
Parameters.Name: IsSingleInstance

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if closed captions will be displayed, for Teams Video Clips, during playback. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: EnableVideoMessageCaptions

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if chat regulation for internal communication in tenant is allowed. Possible Values: True, False
Parameters.Name: EnableInOrganizationChatControl

Parameters.Description: This setting enables/disables the use of custom emojis and reactions across the whole tenant. Upon enablement, admins and/or users can define a user group that is allowed. Possible Values: True, False
Parameters.Name: CustomEmojis

Parameters.Description: This setting enables/disables the availability of Viva Engage storylines in Teams chats across the whole tenant.
Parameters.Name: Storyline

Parameters.Description: This setting enables/disables MessagingNotes integration across the whole tenant. Possible Values: Disabled, Enabled
Parameters.Name: MessagingNotes

Parameters.Description: This setting enables weaponizable file detection in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: FileTypeCheck

Parameters.Description: This setting enables malicious URL detection in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: UrlReputationCheck

Parameters.Description: This setting enables content-based phishing detection for Teams messages in the tenant.
Parameters.Name: ContentBasedPhishingCheck

Parameters.Description: This setting enables the end users to Report incorrect security detections in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: ReportIncorrectSecurityDetections

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the workload's Admin
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory application to authenticate with.
Parameters.Name: ApplicationId

Parameters.Description: Id of the Azure Active Directory tenant used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Description: Thumbprint of the Azure Active Directory application's authentication certificate to use for authentication.
Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Description: The TeamsMessagingConfiguration determines the messaging settings for users in your tenant.

Parameters.Description: Identity for the teams messaging policy you're modifying. To modify the global policy, use this syntax: -Identity global. To modify a per-user policy, use syntax similar to this: -Identity TeamsMessagingPolicy.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if users can chat with groups (Distribution, M365 and Security groups). Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowChatWithGroup

Parameters.Description: These settings enables, disables updating or fetching custom group chat avatars for the users included in the messaging policy. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowCustomGroupChatAvatars

Parameters.Description: This setting enables/disables showing company name and department name in search results for MTO users. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowExtendedWorkInfoInSearch

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if users with the 'Full permissions' role can delete any group or meeting chat message within their tenant. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowFullChatPermissionUserToDeleteAnyMessage

Parameters.Description: Determines if Giphy images should be displayed that had been already sent or received in chat. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowGiphyDisplay

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if users in a group chat can create and share join links for other users within the organization to join that chat. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowGroupChatJoinLinks

Parameters.Description: Determines if a user is allowed to paste internet-based images in compose. Possible values: True, False
Parameters.Name: AllowPasteInternetImage

Parameters.Description: Determines whether files are automatically shared in external chats. Possible values: Enabled: Files are automatically shared in external chats. Disabled: Files are not automatically shared in external chats.
Parameters.Name: AutoShareFilesInExternalChats

Parameters.Description: Determines the Supervised Chat role of the user. Set this to Full to allow the user to supervise chats. Supervisors have the ability to initiate chats with and invite any user within the environment. Set this to Limited to allow the user to initiate conversations with Full and Limited permissioned users, but not Restricted. Set this to Restricted to block chat creation with anyone other than Full permissioned users.
Parameters.Name: ChatPermissionRole

Parameters.Description: This setting enables the creation of custom emojis and reactions within an organization for the specified policy users.
Parameters.Name: CreateCustomEmojis

Parameters.Description: These settings enable and disable the editing and deletion of custom emojis and reactions for the users included in the messaging policy.
Parameters.Name: DeleteCustomEmojis

Parameters.Description: This setting determines whether a user is allowed to create custom AI-powered backgrounds and images with MS Designer.Possible values are: Enabled, Disabled
Parameters.Name: DesignerForBackgroundsAndImages

Parameters.Description: This setting determines if chat regulation for internal communication in the tenant is allowed. Possible values: BlockingAllowed, BlockingDisallowed
Parameters.Name: InOrganizationChatControl

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to delete messages sent by bots. Possible values are: True, False
Parameters.Name: UsersCanDeleteBotMessages

Parameters.Description: Report inappropriate content.
Parameters.Name: AllowCommunicationComplianceEndUserReporting

Parameters.Description: Determines is Fluid Collaboration should be enabled or not.
Parameters.Name: AllowFluidCollaborate

Parameters.Description: Report a security concern.
Parameters.Name: AllowSecurityEndUserReporting

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post Giphys. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowGiphy

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post memes. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowMemes

Parameters.Description: Determines whether owners are allowed to delete all the messages in their team. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowOwnerDeleteMessage

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to edit their own messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserEditMessage

Parameters.Description: Turn on this setting to let a user get text predictions for chat messages.
Parameters.Name: AllowSmartCompose

Parameters.Description: Turn this setting on to enable suggested replies for chat messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowSmartReply

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post stickers. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowStickers

Parameters.Description: Use this setting to turn automatic URL previewing on or off in messages. Set this to TRUE to turn on. Set this to FALSE to turn off.
Parameters.Name: AllowUrlPreviews

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to chat. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to chat across private chat, group chat and in meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit all chat.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserChat

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to delete their own messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserDeleteMessage

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to translate messages to their client languages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowUserTranslation

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to use Immersive Reader for reading conversation messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowImmersiveReader

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to remove a user from a conversation. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowRemoveUser

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to send priorities messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.
Parameters.Name: AllowPriorityMessages

Parameters.Description: Turn this setting on to allow users to permanently delete their 1:1, group chat, and meeting chat as participants (this deletes the chat only for them, not other users in the chat).
Parameters.Name: AllowUserDeleteChat

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to send video messages in Chat. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to send video messages. Set this to FALSE to prohibit sending video messages.
Parameters.Name: AllowVideoMessages

Parameters.Description: Provide a description of your policy to identify purpose of creating it.
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: Determines the Giphy content restrictions applicable to a user. Set this to STRICT, MODERATE or NORESTRICTION.
Parameters.Name: GiphyRatingType

Parameters.Description: Use this setting to specify whether read receipts are user controlled, enabled for everyone, or disabled. Set this to UserPreference, Everyone or None.
Parameters.Name: ReadReceiptsEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Possible values are: DisabledUserOverride,EnabledUserOverride.
Parameters.Name: ChannelsInChatListEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Determines whether a user is allowed to send audio messages. Possible values are: ChatsAndChannels,ChatsOnly,Disabled.
Parameters.Name: AudioMessageEnabledType

Parameters.Description: Indicates whether B2B invites should be used to add external users when necessary. Possible values: Enabled: External users will be added using B2B invites. Disabled: External users will not be added using B2B invites.
Parameters.Name: UseB2BInvitesToAddExternalUsers

Parameters.Description: Globally unique identifier (GUID) of the tenant account whose external user communication policy are being created.
Parameters.Name: Tenant

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the Team Message Policy exists, absent ensures it is removed
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity

Parameters.Description: When set to WifiOnly, prohibits the user from making and receiving video calls or enabling video in meetings using VoIP calls on the mobile device while on a cellular data connection. Possible values are: WifiOnly, AllNetworks.

Parameters.Description: Determines the mobile dialer preference, possible values are: Teams, Native, UserOverride.

Description: This resources configures a Teams Network Roaming Policy.

Parameters.Description: Identity of the Teams Online Voicemail Policy.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Controls if editing call answer rule settings are enabled or disabled for a user. Possible values are $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: EnableEditingCallAnswerRulesSetting

Parameters.Description: Allows you to disable or enable voicemail transcription. Possible values are $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: EnableTranscription

Parameters.Description: Allows you to disable or enable profanity masking for the voicemail transcriptions. Possible values are $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: EnableTranscriptionProfanityMasking

Parameters.Description: Allows you to disable or enable translation for the voicemail transcriptions. Possible values are $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: EnableTranscriptionTranslation

Parameters.Description: A duration of voicemail maximum recording length. The length should be between 30 seconds to 600 seconds.
Parameters.Name: MaximumRecordingLength

Parameters.Description: The audio file to play to the caller after the user's voicemail greeting has played and before the caller is allowed to leave a voicemail message.
Parameters.Name: PostambleAudioFile

Parameters.Description: The audio file to play to the caller before the user's voicemail greeting is played.
Parameters.Name: PreambleAudioFile

Parameters.Description: Is playing the Pre- or Post-amble mandatory before the caller can leave a message. Possible values are $true or $false.
Parameters.Name: PreamblePostambleMandatory

Parameters.Description: The primary (or first) language that voicemail system prompts will be presented in. Must also set SecondarySystemPromptLanguage. When set, this overrides the user language choice.
Parameters.Name: PrimarySystemPromptLanguage

Parameters.Description: The secondary language that voicemail system prompts will be presented in. Must also set PrimarySystemPromptLanguage and may not be the same value as PrimarySystemPromptanguage. When set, this overrides the user language choice.
Parameters.Name: SecondarySystemPromptLanguage

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether voicemail and transcription data are shared with the service for training and improving accuracy. Possible values are Defer and Deny.
Parameters.Name: ShareData

Description:
This resource configures the Teams Online Voicemail Policies.

More information: <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/manage-voicemail-policies/>

Parameters.Description: The Identity parameter represents the ID of the specific user in your organization; this can be either a SIP URI or an Object ID.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: The CallAnswerRule parameter represents the value of the call answer rule, which can be any of the following: DeclineCall, PromptOnly, PromptOnlyWithTransfer, RegularVoicemail, VoicemailWithTransferOption.
Parameters.Name: CallAnswerRule

Parameters.Description: The DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite parameter represents the contents that overwrite the default normal greeting prompt. If the user's normal custom greeting is not set and DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite is not empty, the voicemail service will play this overwrite greeting instead of the default normal greeting in the voicemail deposit scenario.
Parameters.Name: DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite

Parameters.Description: The DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite parameter represents the contents that overwrite the default out-of-office greeting prompt. If the user's out-of-office custom greeting is not set and DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite is not empty, the voicemail service will play this overwrite greeting instead of the default out-of-office greeting in the voicemail deposit scenario.
Parameters.Name: DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite

Parameters.Description: The OofGreetingEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario.
Parameters.Name: OofGreetingEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OofGreetingFollowAutomaticRepliesEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario when user set automatic replies in Outlook.
Parameters.Name: OofGreetingFollowAutomaticRepliesEnabled

Parameters.Description: The OofGreetingFollowCalendarEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario when user set out-of-office in calendar.
Parameters.Name: OofGreetingFollowCalendarEnabled

Parameters.Description: The PromptLanguage parameter represents the language that is used to play voicemail prompts.
Parameters.Name: PromptLanguage

Parameters.Description: Specifies whether voicemail and transcription data is shared with the service for training and improving accuracy.
Parameters.Name: ShareData

Parameters.Description: The TransferTarget parameter represents the target to transfer the call when call answer rule set to PromptOnlyWithTransfer or VoicemailWithTransferOption. Value of this parameter should be a SIP URI of another user in your organization. For user with Enterprise Voice enabled, a valid telephone number could also be accepted as TransferTarget.
Parameters.Name: TransferTarget

Parameters.Description: The VoicemailEnabled parameter represents whether to enable voicemail service. If set to $false, the user has no voicemail service.
Parameters.Name: VoicemailEnabled

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the policy exists, absent ensures it is removed.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Name: Credential

Description: This resource configures a Teams User's Online Voicemail Settings.

Parameters.Description: Specifies the identity of the target user.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: Specifies the unique identifier of the emergency location to assign to the user. Location identities can be discovered by using the Get-CsOnlineLisLocation cmdlet.

Parameters.Description: The DownloadTeams property allows admins to control whether the Skype for Business client should automatically download Teams in the background. This Boolean setting is only honored on Windows clients, and only for certain values of the user's TeamsUpgradePolicy. If NotifySfbUser=true or if Mode=TeamsOnly in TeamsUpgradePolicy, this setting is honored. Otherwise it is ignored.
Parameters.Name: DownloadTeams

Parameters.Description: The SfBMeetingJoinUx property allows admins to specify which app is used to join Skype for Business meetings, even after the user has been upgraded to Teams. Allowed values are: 'SkypeMeetingsApp' and 'NativeLimitedClient'. 'NativeLimitedClient' means the existing Skype for Business rich client will be used, but since the user is upgraded, only meeting functionality is available. Calling and Messaging are done via Teams. 'SkypeMeetingsApp' means use the web-downloadable app. This setting can be useful for organizations that have upgraded to Teams and no longer want to install Skype for Business on their users' computers.
Parameters.Name: SfBMeetingJoinUx

Parameters.Description: The time the call will ring the user before it is forwarded to the unanswered target. The supported format is hh:mm:ss and the delay range needs to be between 10 and 60 seconds in 10 seconds increments, i.e. 00:00:10, 00:00:20, 00:00:30, 00:00:40, 00:00:50 and 00:01:00. The default value is 20 seconds.
Parameters.Name: UnansweredDelay

Parameters.Description: The unanswered target. Supported type of values are ObjectId, SIP address and phone number. For phone numbers we support the following types of formats: E.164 (+12065551234 or +1206555000;ext=1234) or non-E.164 like 1234.

Parameters.Description: The type of forwarding to set. Supported values are Immediate and Simultaneous
Parameters.Name: ForwardingType

Parameters.Description: The forwarding target type. Supported values are Voicemail, SingleTarget, MyDelegates and Group. Voicemail is only supported for Immediate forwarding.
Parameters.Name: ForwardingTargetType

Parameters.Description: The forwarding target. Supported types of values are ObjectId's, SIP addresses and phone numbers. For phone numbers we support the following types of formats: E.164 (+12065551234 or +1206555000;ext=1234) or non-E.164 like 1234.

Description: This resource implements Teams VDI Policies.

Parameters.Description: Identity of the Teams Voice Route.
Parameters.Name: Identity

Parameters.Description: BridgeSourcePhoneNumber is an E.164 formatted Operator Connect Conferencing phone number assigned to your Audio Conferencing Bridge. Using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber in an online voice route is mutually exclusive with using OnlinePstnGatewayList in the same online voice route. When using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber in an online voice route, the OnlinePstnUsages used in the online voice route should only be used in a corresponding OnlineAudioConferencingRoutingPolicy. The same OnlinePstnUsages should not be used in online voice routes that are not using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber.
Parameters.Name: BridgeSourcePhoneNumber

Parameters.Description: A description of what this online voice route is for.
Parameters.Name: Description

Parameters.Description: A regular expression that specifies the phone numbers to which this route applies. Numbers matching this pattern will be routed according to the rest of the routing settings.
Parameters.Name: NumberPattern

Parameters.Description: This parameter contains a list of online gateways associated with this online voice route.  Each member of this list must be the service Identity of the online PSTN gateway.
Parameters.Name: OnlinePstnGatewayList

Parameters.Description: A list of online PSTN usages (such as Local, Long Distance, etc.) that can be applied to this online voice route. The PSTN usage must be an existing usage (PSTN usages can be retrieved by calling the Get-CsOnlinePstnUsage cmdlet).
Parameters.Name: OnlinePstnUsages

Parameters.Description: A number could resolve to multiple online voice routes. The priority determines the order in which the routes will be applied if more than one route is possible.
Parameters.Name: Priority

Parameters.Description: Present ensures the route exists, absent ensures it is removed.
Parameters.Name: Ensure

Parameters.Description: Credentials of the Teams Admin
Parameters.Name: Credential

Parameters.Name: TenantId

Parameters.Name: CertificateThumbprint

Parameters.Description: Managed ID being used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: ManagedIdentity

Parameters.Description: Access token used for authentication.
Parameters.Name: AccessTokens
